question: Which of the following has the lowest bearing capacity?
keyMemory: Sa ALE, kapag usapan ay pinakamahinang natural foundation soil, madalas lumalabas si peat bilang pinakamababa ang bearing capacity.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Peat
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Peat.
Bakit peat? Kasi ang bearing capacity ay kakayahan ng lupa na buhatin ang load ng structure nang hindi sobra ang settlement o hindi bumibigay ang soil. Sa apat na choices, peat ang pinakamahina dahil organic soil ito, sobrang compressible, madalas basa, spongy, at unstable. Iyan ang klase ng lupa na ayaw na ayaw mong gawing direct foundation support kung walang special treatment.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag nakita mo ang word na lowest bearing capacity, hanapin mo agad ang soil na soft, organic, wet, compressible. Iyon si peat.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Clay puwedeng problematic kapag expansive o soft, pero hindi siya automatic na pinakamababa sa listahan.
Gravel with sand usually mas maganda ang bearing dahil dense at better drained.
Escombro fill material o rubble fill iyan, hindi ideal bilang natural bearing soil, pero sa choices na ito, peat pa rin ang pinakaworst.
Elimination method:
Tanggalin mo agad ang gravel with sand kasi alam mong mas matibay iyan kaysa soft soils.
Then compare clay, escombro, at peat.
Kapag may peat, kadalasan siya ang red-flag answer sa geotech question.
So kahit nag-aalangan ka, doon ka na.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE, kapag usapan ay pinakamahinang natural foundation soil, madalas lumalabas si peat bilang pinakamababa ang bearing capacity.

question: To determine the bearing capacity of the foundation-bed, the following are applied:
keyMemory: Kung ang tanong ay paano malalaman ang bearing capacity, ang sagot ay hindi tamping o grading. Ang sagot ay test boring / soil investigation.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Test borings
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Test borings.
Ito ang mahalagang distinction: ang tanong ay to determine the bearing capacity. Hindi niya tinatanong kung paano pagandahin ang lupa. Ang tinatanong niya ay paano mo malalaman o ia-assess ang capacity ng foundation bed.
Ang tamang sagot ay test borings dahil ito ang actual subsurface investigation method. Dito mo malalaman ang soil profile, lalim ng strata, klase ng lupa, moisture condition, at minsan pati estimated bearing behavior. Sa Philippine practice, dito pumapasok ang soil exploration, test boring, at kapag kailangan, SPT.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Soil tamping ay improvement or compaction measure. Hindi ito pangunahing paraan para malaman ang bearing capacity.
Soil grading ay pag-aayos ng slope o site levels. Hindi ito geotechnical investigation method.
All of the above common trap ito. Mukhang comprehensive, pero dalawa sa choices hindi talaga pang-determination. Kaya automatic bagsak na ang “all of the above.”
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang verb sa tanong: determine.
Kapag “determine,” isipin mo investigation, testing, boring, analysis.
Kapag ang choice ay improvement lang tulad ng tamping or grading, tanggal agad.
Common ALE trap ito:
Nililito ka sa pagitan ng site preparation at soil investigation. Magkaiba iyon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kung ang tanong ay paano malalaman ang bearing capacity, ang sagot ay hindi tamping o grading. Ang sagot ay test boring / soil investigation.

question: In green architecture, what is the preferred site for a community?
keyMemory: Sa green architecture, brown site ang mas preferred kaysa sirain ang bagong greenfield land.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Brown site
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Brown site.
Sa sustainable or green architecture, mas preferred ang brown site o brownfield kaysa untouched green site. Bakit? Kasi ang prinsipyo ng green design ay hindi lang tungkol sa paglalagay ng halaman. Kasama rito ang responsible land use. Mas gusto mong i-redevelop ang dati nang nagamit, napabayaan, o urbanized na site kaysa sirain ang virgin land o open greenfield.
Ito ang clue:
Kapag sinabing green architecture, huwag ka agad mauto sa salitang “green site.” Common trap iyan. Hindi porke may word na green, iyon na ang sustainable answer.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Green site sounds attractive, pero ang problem diyan ay often undeveloped or natural land iyan. Sustainable planning usually tries to preserve those.
All of the above mali dahil hindi parehong preferred ang green at brown site.
None of the above obvious na mali dahil may isang choice na pasok talaga.
Elimination method:
Watch out sa board trap na keyword echo.
Question says green architecture, tapos may choice na green site. Mukhang bagay pakinggan, pero iyon mismo ang trap.
Ang mas malalim na sustainability logic: reuse first before disturb new land.
Philippine ALE logic:
Sa local professional practice, lalo na sa site planning at sustainability discussions, mas pabor sa redevelopment, adaptive reuse, infill development, at paggamit ng previously disturbed land.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa green architecture, brown site ang mas preferred kaysa sirain ang bagong greenfield land.

question: The determination of the character of the materials, study of sub-surface irregularities done by an architect is called:
keyMemory: Site investigation ang kabuuang pag-aaral. Test boring ay isa lang sa tools o methods sa loob nito.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Site investigation
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Site investigation.
Ang description sa tanong ay malawak: determination of materials, study of subsurface irregularities. Ibig sabihin, overall process ito ng pag-aaral sa site. Kaya ang tamang umbrella term ay site investigation.
Test borings ay isa lang sa mga methods na ginagamit sa site investigation. Ibig sabihin, mas specific siya, hindi siya ang pinakamalawak na sagot. Sa ALE, kapag may broad term at may mas specific term sa choices, kadalasan ang tamang sagot ay iyong sakop ang buong description ng tanong.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Test borings too narrow. Parte lang ito ng investigation.
Site work actual construction activity na sa site, hindi study phase.
Site check masyadong informal ang dating; hindi ito ang tamang technical term dito.
Elimination method:
Tanungin mo ang sarili mo: “Ang description ba ay buong process, o isang method lang?”
Dito buong process. Kaya piliin ang broad but accurate term: site investigation.
Common trap:
Kapag nakakita ka ng specific technical word gaya ng test boring, parang mas mukhang matalino. Pero hindi lagi iyon ang tamang sagot. Dapat sakto sa scope ng tanong.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Site investigation ang kabuuang pag-aaral. Test boring ay isa lang sa tools o methods sa loob nito.

question: As per PD 1096, the minimum number of floors of a building that is required to submit a Standard Penetration Test or Test Boring Report
keyMemory: Sa ALE, tandaan mo itong memory peg: Soil boring / SPT report — 3 storeys and above ang usual answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 3
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 3 floors, or three storeys.
Ito ang board-type code memory question. Sa ALE logic and under the NBCP IRR / related building permit structural requirements, buildings or structures of three storeys and higher are the usual threshold where boring tests and, if needed, load tests are required. Kaya sa choices, ang best answer ay 3. (VIZCODE)
Bakit hindi 1 or 2?
Kasi sobrang broad naman noon kung lahat ng 1-storey at 2-storey automatic kailangan agad ng SPT or boring report under this reviewer logic. May cases na puwedeng kailangan pa rin due to geohazards or special conditions, pero ang minimum code threshold na gusto ng item ay 3 storeys. (VIZCODE)
Bakit hindi 4?
Ito ang common trap. May mga local practices o misconceptions na parang mas “heavy building” lang ang 4-storey ang kailangan. Pero itong item, as framed under PD 1096 reviewer logic, ang cutoff ay 3 storeys and higher. (VIZCODE)
Elimination method:
Kapag may code-style item at choices ay 1, 2, 3, 4, tanungin mo: “Ano ang pinaka-kilalang threshold sa Philippine building permit practice?”
Madalas lumalabas ang 3-storey and above sa structural and soil investigation requirements.
So ang natitira: 3.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE, tandaan mo itong memory peg: Soil boring / SPT report — 3 storeys and above ang usual answer.

question: Stakes and batter board in a construction lay outing procedure refers to

choices.text: Building components where permanent horizontal and vertical measurement are indicated
Vertical and horizontal wood sticks and lumber used to determine the elevation and distances of the reference points of the proposed building

answer: c. Vertical and horizontal wood sticks and lumber used to determine the elevation and distances of the reference points of the proposed building
keyMemory: Stakes at batter boards ay temporary layout tools para sa reference lines, distance, at elevation ng proposed building.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Vertical and horizontal wood sticks and lumber used to determine the elevation and distances of the reference points of the proposed building
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay choice c.
Ito ang literal at technical description ng stakes and batter boards. Ginagamit sila sa layouting para mailipat mo sa actual site ang building lines, offsets, at reference elevations. Hindi pa ito permanent building component. Hindi rin ito formwork.
Bakit tama ang c?
Kasi may dalawang mahalagang functions na binanggit:
una, elevation
ikalawa, distances/reference points
Iyan mismo ang purpose ng stakes at batter boards sa site layout.
Bakit mali ang iba:
a mali dahil sinabi niyang permanent. Eh stakes and batter boards are temporary layout devices.
b mali dahil formwork is for concrete shaping, not for layout reference.
d obviously wrong, parang joke choice iyan.
Elimination method:
Kapag may tanong sa layout tools, tingnan kung ang choice ba ay temporary reference aid or actual building part.
Kapag actual building part ang dating, tanggalin mo.
Kapag pang-layout at pang-reference, iyon ang piliin.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Stakes at batter boards ay temporary layout tools para sa reference lines, distance, at elevation ng proposed building.

question: What is the vertical location of a ground surface often used as a reference point with respect to vertical height?
keyMemory: Grade = level of the ground surface. Elevation = general vertical height of a point. Magkaiba sila.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Grade
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Grade.
Medyo tricky ang wording nito. Ang hinahanap niya ay iyong vertical location ng ground surface. Ang tawag diyan sa construction terms ay grade. Sa madaling sabi, iyan ang level ng lupa or ground line na madalas ginagamit na reference.
Bakit hindi elevation?
Kasi elevation is a more general term. Puwedeng elevation ng floor, elevation ng roof, elevation ng point, elevation from datum.
Pero ang tanong ay very specifically tungkol sa ground surface. Kaya mas sakto ang grade.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Batter board tool iyan, hindi descriptive term ng ground level.
Bench mark fixed point with known elevation iyon, reference point siya pero hindi siya mismo ang vertical location ng ground surface.
Elevation too broad; hindi as precise as grade for this item.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay may clue na ground surface, isipin mo agad grade.
Kapag known fixed point with exact elevation, bench mark iyon.
Kapag general vertical measurement from datum, elevation iyon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Grade = level of the ground surface.
Elevation = general vertical height of a point.
Magkaiba sila.

question: The horizontal boards usually of lumber used to establish the height of the finish foundation and to support the guidelines for the excavation of the footing trenches is called:
keyMemory: Sa footing layout at trench alignment, ang horizontal board na reference mo ay batter board.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Batter boards
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Batter boards.
Ito ang classic definition ng batter boards sa construction layout. Horizontal lumber boards sila na nakamount sa stakes, at ginagamit para hawakan ang string lines na nagseset ng building line at footing trench alignment. Dito mo rin nase-set ang reference height.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Stake outs refers more to the act or process of setting out points, hindi iyong actual horizontal board.
Level boards puwedeng pakinggan na malapit, pero ang standard construction term sa ganitong gamit ay batter boards.
Box boards hindi ito ang recognized standard answer dito.
Elimination method:
Key words: horizontal boards, support guidelines, excavation of footing trenches.
Iyan ang signature description ng batter boards.
Kapag nakita mo ang “support string lines during layout,” malapit na agad kay batter board.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa footing layout at trench alignment, ang horizontal board na reference mo ay batter board.

question: The Tagalog equivalent of earth fill is:
keyMemory: Sa old local construction usage, escombro ang reviewer answer para sa earth fill / filling material.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. escombro
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Escombro.
Ito ay old-school local construction terminology question. Sa reviewer language at sa maraming local jobsite terms, escombro ang ginagamit para sa fill material o pantambak. Hindi man ito purong Tagalog sa academic sense, ito ang construction term na tinutukoy ng item.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Estaka means stake. Pang-layout iyon.
Banda y banda hindi earth fill iyon.
Larga masa hindi rin ito ang ginagamit na term para sa earth fill sa ganitong context.
Elimination method:
Kapag hindi mo sure ang exact term, gumamit ka ng association method:
Estaka obvious na layout term. Tanggal.
Banda y banda tunog positional o directional. Tanggal.
Natitira ang escombro at larga masa.
Sa actual construction vocabulary, mas associated ang escombro sa tambak/fill/rubble fill material.
Common trap ito dahil linguistic ang dating, hindi pure technical.
Kaya minsan kailangan mong alalahanin ang site vocabulary at hindi lang formal code terms.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa old local construction usage, escombro ang reviewer answer para sa earth fill / filling material.

question: One method of leveling batterboards without the transit is the use of

choices.text: ¼ inch plastic hose filled with water

answer: a. ¼ inch plastic hose filled with water
keyMemory: Kung walang transit, puwedeng gumamit ng water hose level para magpantay ng batter boards.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. ¼ inch plastic hose filled with water
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay iyong plastic hose filled with water.
Ito ang tinatawag na water level principle. Kahit wala kang transit, puwede mong mailipat ang same elevation from one point to another gamit ang tubig sa transparent hose. Very practical ito sa site, lalo na sa simpler setups.
Bakit tama ito?
Kasi ang tubig naturally settles at equal level sa dalawang dulo, kaya puwede mo siyang gamitin para pantayin ang batter boards.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Eyesight hindi reliable for accurate leveling.
Plumb bob pang-check ng verticality, hindi pang-level ng horizontal reference.
Meter stick pangsukat lang, hindi siya level-transfer instrument by itself.
Elimination method:
Keyword: leveling without transit.
So hanapin mo ang substitute ng leveling instrument.
Kapag choice ay vertical tool or measuring tool lang, tanggal agad.
Water hose ang only choice na talagang pang-transfer ng level.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kung walang transit, puwedeng gumamit ng water hose level para magpantay ng batter boards.

question: A vertical line check uses a
keyMemory: Vertical = plumb bob. Ito ang automatic mental pairing mo sa exam.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Plumb bob
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Plumb bob.
Simple pero favorite sa board. Ang plumb bob ay ginagamit para macheck ang true vertical line dahil sumusunod ito sa gravity. Kapag verticality ang usapan, plumb bob agad ang isipin mo.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Meter stick pangsukat lang.
Tri-square ginagamit for checking right angles or squareness, not full vertical line in construction alignment.
Water hose pang-level transfer iyon, not plumbing vertical line.
Elimination method:
Keyword: vertical line check.
Vertical = plumb.
Horizontal = level.
Right angle = square.
Kapag namaster mo itong tatlong pairing na ito, mabilis ka sa elimination.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Vertical = plumb bob.
Ito ang automatic mental pairing mo sa exam.

question: Instrument or tool capable of vertical and horizontal line check is called
keyMemory: Spirit level ang tool for checking both level and plumb, depending on how you place it.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Spirit level
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Spirit level.
Ang spirit level may vial or bubble. Depende sa orientation ng tool, puwede nitong i-check ang horizontal at vertical alignment. Kaya siya ang tamang sagot.
Bakit hindi plumb bob?
Kasi plumb bob is mainly for vertical only. Hindi siya pang-horizontal check.
Bakit hindi water hose?
Water hose is for level transfer, not a general vertical-and-horizontal checking tool.
Meter stick, obvious, pangsukat lang.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo ang tanong: capable of vertical and horizontal line check.
Ibig sabihin versatile tool ang hanap.
Sa choices, tanging spirit level lang ang pasok sa parehong gamit.
Common trap:
Maraming examinee ang nadadala sa plumb bob dahil narinig nila vertical. Pero kulang iyon kasi hinahanap ng tanong ay parehong vertical at horizontal.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Spirit level ang tool for checking both level and plumb, depending on how you place it.

question: Before staking and lay-outing, the first thing to do is:
keyMemory: Sa layouting, verify control/reference points first bago ka mag-stake at maglatag ng building lines.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Check points
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Check points.
Medyo old reviewer wording ito, pero ALE logic: bago ka mag-staking at layout, kailangan mo munang i-verify ang reference points, property corners, control points, at benchmarks na gagamitin mo. Hindi ka puwedeng mag-layout nang wala kang pinanggagalingang verified points.
Bakit hindi slope muna?
Ang slope mahalaga, pero hindi iyon ang pinakaunang control step sa actual setting out.
Bakit hindi elevation agad?
Kailangan mo muna ng checked points para alam mo kung saan mo itatali ang elevation reference.
Bakit hindi road elevation?
Mahalaga iyan sa planning at compliance, pero sa tanong na “before staking and lay-outing,” mas basic at mas primary ang checking points.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay first thing to do, hanapin ang pinaka-basic na prerequisite.
Hindi ka makaka-check ng slope, road elevation, or accurate layout kung hindi mo pa verified ang points mo.
So check points ang foundation ng lahat.
Common trap:
Pinipili ng iba ang check elevation kasi mukhang technical. Pero sequence-wise, una muna ang control points.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa layouting, verify control/reference points first bago ka mag-stake at maglatag ng building lines.

question: What is the minimum depth of excavation of soil from its natural level?
keyMemory: Memory peg: minimum footing excavation depth from natural level = 0.60 m sa basic reviewer logic.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 0.60 meter
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 0.60 meter.
Sa basic building construction review, itong 0.60 m below natural ground level ang common minimum footing excavation depth na lumalabas sa ALE-type reviewers. Ang idea rito ay mailagay ang foundation sa mas stable na level, below top loose soil and surface effects.
Bakit hindi 0.40?
Masyadong mababaw iyan para sa common minimum footing embedment.
Bakit hindi 0.90 or 1.00?
Puwedeng mas malalim sa actual design depende sa load at soil condition, pero ang tanong ay minimum, kaya 0.60 m ang reviewer answer.
Elimination method:
Keyword: minimum depth.
So huwag pipili ng sobrang laki kung may mas standard, mas basic minimum value sa choices.
Sa apat, ang pinakafamiliar basic construction minimum ay 0.60 meter.
Important reminder:
Sa totoong practice, final depth still depends on soil condition, structural load, and engineer’s design. Pero sa ALE reviewer format, 0.60 m ang dapat tandaan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Memory peg: minimum footing excavation depth from natural level = 0.60 m sa basic reviewer logic.

question: What is the key factor in determining what would give the most benefits in good site-development plan?
keyMemory: Sa site development, topography ang isa sa pinakaunang binabasa dahil siya ang malakas magdikta ng layout, drainage, access, at cost.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Topography
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Topography.
Medyo vague ang wording, pero sa site development, ang pinaka-driving physical factor ay topography. Bakit? Kasi dito naka-base ang drainage, grading, cut and fill, access, building placement, retaining requirements, orientation opportunities, at minsan pati cost ng development. Kung mali ang basa mo sa topography, sablay ang buong site-development plan.
Bakit hindi users?
Users are very important in planning, yes. Pero ang tanong ay tungkol sa site-development plan, at dito physical site conditions are often the strongest determinant of what is practical and beneficial on the ground.
Bakit hindi local gov’t laws?
Constraint iyon, not the key planning generator of site benefit.
Bakit hindi building and its links?
Masyadong secondary iyan kung hindi mo pa naiintindihan ang site itself.
Elimination method:
Kapag “site-development plan” ang keyword, unahin mo ang site-driven factors.
Ano ang pinaka-site-driven sa choices? Topography.
Iyan ang dahilan bakit siya natitira.
Common trap:
Yung choice na users sounds humane and nice, pero mas bagay iyan sa programming and design planning in general.
Sa site development, physical land condition ang mas primary.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa site development, topography ang isa sa pinakaunang binabasa dahil siya ang malakas magdikta ng layout, drainage, access, at cost.

question: What is used as reference line in establishing a floor elevation or height?

choices.text: FFL of NGL (Finished Floor Line of Natural Grade Line)

keyMemory: Sa site and elevation questions, madalas na ang ground or natural grade line ang pinakabase reference mo sa vertical relationship ng building.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Natural grade line
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Natural grade line.
Sa ALE logic, kapag ang tanong ay ano ang reference line sa pag-establish ng floor elevation or height, ang base line mo ay usually ang natural grade line o ground reference. Sa building documents and code context, grade or natural ground level is a major reference for vertical measurements and site-related elevations. (VIZCODE)
Bakit hindi street level?
Mahalaga ang street level for some site decisions, especially drainage and curb relation, pero hindi iyon ang general reference line na gusto ng item.
Bakit hindi GFL?
Ground floor line or ground floor level can be an established design level already, pero hindi siya ang base reference na pinanggagalingan ng pag-establish.
Bakit hindi FFL of NGL?
Mukhang ginawang complicated phrase lang. Hindi ito ang standard straightforward answer sa tanong.
Elimination method:
Watch the phrase reference line.
Ang hinahanap ay iyong base or datum-like site line, hindi iyong resulting floor line.
Kaya natural grade line ang pinaka-logical.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa site and elevation questions, madalas na ang ground or natural grade line ang pinakabase reference mo sa vertical relationship ng building.

answerConceptVisual.title: Floor elevation reference
answerConceptVisual.detail: The reference line is the natural ground/grade line.

question: with the use of plumb bob

choices.text: 2-3-4 multiples with the use of transit
3-4-5 multiples with the use of steel tape measure
3-5-7 multiples with the use of plumb bob

answer: b. 3-4-5 multiples with the use of steel tape measure
keyMemory: Kapag right angle sa layout, automatic memory mo dapat: 3-4-5.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 3-4-5 multiples with the use of steel tape measure
Ulit: 3-4-5 multiples with steel tape measure.
So ngayon malinaw na bakit mali ang c at d.
Ang tamang pang-square sa field ay 3-4-5 rule, dahil iyon ang classic Pythagorean right triangle. Yung 4-5-4 hindi valid right-triangle proportion, at yung 3-5-7 lalo nang hindi. Tapos trap din yung “with the use of plumb bob” kasi plumb bob is for verticality, hindi pang-manual square ng corners. Kaya kahit makita mo pa ang missing c at d, hindi pa rin gagalaw ang sagot. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag right angle sa layout, automatic memory mo dapat: 3-4-5.

question: In lay-outing the road system in a subdivision with slopes of more than 30 degrees, what is the most economical method to use?
keyMemory: Sa steep subdivision slopes, reviewer logic: grading ang mas economical kaysa aggressive cut-and-fill.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. grading
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay grading.
Ito ang logic: sa matarik na site, lalo na more than 30 degrees, cut and fill can become expensive and disruptive. Kaya ang mas economical approach ay grading, and then sa next question makikita mo kung anong klaseng grading ang mas advisable: parallel with the slope. Iyan ang clue kung bakit hindi cut and fill ang gusto ng reviewer. (Quizlet)
Elimination method:
Una, tanggalin mo ang all of the above kasi hindi puwedeng parehong “grading” at “cut and fill” ang most economical sa iisang tanong.
Pangalawa, kapag steep site, magduda ka sa cut and fill kasi iyan ang madalas magpalaki ng excavation at retaining problems.
So natitira: grading.
Common ALE trap ito:
Kapag nakita mo ang salitang “road system,” minsan gusto mong piliin ang mas engineering-sounding na cut and fill. Pero ang tanong ay most economical.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa steep subdivision slopes, reviewer logic: grading ang mas economical kaysa aggressive cut-and-fill.

question: In grading the site, the most advisable method is:
answer: a. grading parallel with the slope
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, grading parallel with the slope = mas advisable dahil mas kaunti ang forced alteration ng terrain.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. grading parallel with the slope
Ulit: grading parallel with the slope.
Ito ang magandang tandem sa previous question. Kung gusto mong maging economical at less disruptive sa natural terrain, ang principle ay sumunod hangga’t maaari sa existing slope instead of fighting it too much. Kaya parallel with the slope ang reviewer answer. (Quizlet)
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Sa site development, laging tanong mo, “Ano ang mas less earthwork? Ano ang mas kaunting galaw ng lupa?” Usually, ang sagot ay iyong mas sumusunod sa natural topography.
Bakit mali ang iba:
Perpendicular/against the slope usually mas malakas ang intervention mo sa site.
None at all obvious distractors na lang iyan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, grading parallel with the slope = mas advisable dahil mas kaunti ang forced alteration ng terrain.

answerConceptVisual.title: Grading method
answerConceptVisual.detail: Economical grading follows the terrain instead of fighting the natural slope.

question: The correct Filipino term for “stake out” the plot or site is:
keyMemory: Estaka = stake. I-estaka / iiestaka = to stake out.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. iiestaka
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay iiestaka.
Medyo luma at jobsite-vocabulary type ito. Ang clue dito ay ang phrase na “stake out” ay action or verb.
So:
•	estaka = the stake itself, parang noun
•	iiestaka / i-estaka = the act of staking out, kaya ito ang mas tamang reviewer answer
Bakit hindi estakahan?
Kasi mas tunog place, system, o area ng estaka, hindi mismong action.
Bakit hindi estaka?
Dahil object iyon, hindi action.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo agad ang grammar ng tanong.
Kapag ang hinahanap ay gagawin o action, huwag pipili ng noun kung may verb-like choice.
Board exam trap ito:
Technical question pero ang susi ay language form.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Estaka = stake.
I-estaka / iiestaka = to stake out.

question: In lay-outing the cranes in a site, what is considered in their installation?
keyMemory: Sa crane layout, isipin mo lagi: abutin ba, aabot ba sa taas, at saan ligtas itatayo?
explanation:
Correct answer: d. All of the above
Ulit: all of the above.
Kapag crane installation ang usapan, hindi puwedeng isang factor lang. Kailangan mong isipin ang reach, height, at foundation/base location. Lahat iyan critical sa actual site planning ng crane. Ang siting ng tower crane, for example, laging may kinalaman sa boom reach, required height, at support/foundation arrangement. (Facebook)
Elimination method:
Kapag ang choices ay puro valid considerations, at walang obviously mali, malakas ang chance na all of the above ang sagot.
Dito, walang out-of-place na option. Kaya huwag matakot pumili ng all of the above kapag justified.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa crane layout, isipin mo lagi: abutin ba, aabot ba sa taas, at saan ligtas itatayo?

question: In construction, what is the most efficient and more hygienic method of the workers’ waste disposal?
keyMemory: Sa site sanitation questions, portalets ang best answer sa ganitong wording.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. portalets
Ulit: portalets.
Ito straightforward. Sa construction site sanitation, ang portalets o portable toilets ang mas hygienic at mas organized kaysa improvised latrines o open pit. Sa actual site practice, iyan ang mas acceptable, controllable, at mas maayos sa health and housekeeping. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Tanggal agad ang open pit kasi unhygienic at primitive.
Then compare latrines vs portalets.
Kung “more hygienic” ang keyword, mas lamang ang portable sanitary units.
Common trap:
May mga tanong na hindi lang “possible” ang hanap kundi best o most hygienic. Doon madalas nadadale ang examinee.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa site sanitation questions, portalets ang best answer sa ganitong wording.

question: In building layout, the reference point is established from?
keyMemory: Kapag horizontal building layout on lot, isipin mo street line / building line / property reference, hindi agad elevation datum.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. street line
Ulit: street line.
Dito ang clue ay building layout or horizontal positioning sa lot. Sa actual setting out, madalas kang kumukuha ng baseline or reference mula sa street line / road boundary / curb-related control line, dahil iyan ang practical and legal site reference para sa setbacks at building placement. Hindi ito elevation question kaya hindi natural grade o sea level ang best fit. (Civil Engineering Blog)
Bakit mali ang iba:
Natural grade line mas gamit sa vertical references at height relations.
Ground floor line resulta na iyan ng design, hindi pinanggagalingang site reference.
Sea level masyadong geodetic at hindi iyon ang practical layout reference sa site.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag horizontal building layout on lot, isipin mo street line / building line / property reference, hindi agad elevation datum.

question: While performing excavation, if the water content of the soil is high, what is best to design the footing?
keyMemory: Sa weak or wet soil MCQ logic, madalas ang sagot ay increase footing area — kaya wider.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. wider
Ulit: wider.
Kapag mataas ang water content, usually bumababa ang effective support behavior ng soil. Ang immediate design logic sa multiple choice na ito ay palawakin ang footing para mas ma-distribute ang load at bumaba ang soil pressure. Kaya wider ang best answer. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Deeper puwedeng totoo sa ibang situations, pero hindi iyon ang pinaka-direct na sagot sa “design the footing” kung ang concern mo ay support area.
Thicker affects the footing member itself, pero hindi agad nito naa-address ang soil pressure.
Tapered shape lang iyon, not the main corrective idea here.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa weak or wet soil MCQ logic, madalas ang sagot ay increase footing area — kaya wider.

question: What will happen if the excavation is not correctly followed?
keyMemory: Maling excavation = madalas additional concrete, dagdag gastos agad.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Increase in concrete volume
Ulit: increase in concrete volume.
Ito practical site question. Kapag sumobra ka sa excavation o mali ang sukat ng hukay, ang usual direct result ay mas maraming concrete ang kakailanganin para mapuno o ma-achieve ang intended section. Kaya additional cost agad iyan. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba:
Increase in rebars hindi automatic.
No effect obviously wrong.
Additional shoring possible sa ibang site safety situation, pero hindi iyon ang pinaka-direct at most common consequence ng mali ang excavation dimensions.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Maling excavation = madalas additional concrete, dagdag gastos agad.

answerConceptVisual.title: Excavation error effect
answerConceptVisual.detail: If trenches/footings are over-excavated, more concrete is needed to fill the extra volume.

question: What needs to be done before pouring concrete for slab on grade.
keyMemory: Sa local reviewer logic, bago slab on grade pour, soil poisoning ang laging lumalabas na answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Soil Poisoning
Ulit: soil poisoning.
Ito ang tipong reviewer item na may Philippine residential construction practice flavor. Sa slab-on-grade, isang very common pre-pour step ang soil poisoning or anti-termite treatment sa treated ground area bago buhusan ang slab. Totoo na importante rin ang compaction, pero sa ganitong reviewer wording, ang gustong idiin ay iyong specific pre-pour treatment under slab. May official and project-spec examples din na naglalagay ng soil poisoning after grading/leveling and before slab-on-grade concreting. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Tanggal agad ang Concrete Neutralizer kasi hindi standard answer.
Between compaction and soil poisoning, tandaan mo: kung reviewer-style at slab-on-grade ang tanong, madalas ang hinahanap ay anti-termite soil treatment.
Soil grading mas maaga pang site prep iyan.
Common trap ito:
Pipiliin mo ang structurally logical na compaction. Hindi siya mali sa totoong construction sequence, pero sa reviewer intent, soil poisoning ang mas specific expected answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa local reviewer logic, bago slab on grade pour, soil poisoning ang laging lumalabas na answer.

question: Closely set piles of timber, reinforced or pre-stressed concrete or steel driven vertically into the ground to keep earth or water out of an excavation?
keyMemory: Sheet piles = pang-harang ng lupa at tubig sa excavation.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. sheet piles
Ulit: sheet piles.
Very definition-based ito. Ang sheet piles ay interlocking members driven close together para gumawa ng barrier laban sa soil at water intrusion sa excavation. Iyan mismo ang description ng item. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kapag ang clue ay keep earth or water out of excavation, isipin mo agad barrier wall system, hindi load-bearing pile lang.
So alisin mo ang bored piles at concrete piles kung ang emphasis ay retention/exclusion, not just support.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sheet piles = pang-harang ng lupa at tubig sa excavation.

question: A barrier or diaphragm formed to prevent the movement of soil to stabilize foundation, etc. is
keyMemory: Kapag narinig mo ang reviewer phrase na barrier or diaphragm to prevent soil movement, malakas ang answer na sheet piling.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Sheet piling
Ulit: sheet piling.
Ito halos kapatid ng previous question. Sa reviewer definitions, sheet piling ang tawag sa barrier or diaphragm na pumipigil sa galaw ng lupa para ma-stabilize ang excavation or foundation area. (Studocu)
Bakit mali ang iba:
Shear wall pang-lateral load resistance ng building frame.
Retaining wall humahawak ng lupa, yes, pero ang exact reviewer phrase na “barrier or diaphragm” points more specifically to sheet piling.
Riprap pang-erosion protection, hindi diaphragm.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag narinig mo ang reviewer phrase na barrier or diaphragm to prevent soil movement, malakas ang answer na sheet piling.

question: Pressure exerted against the underground portion of a building created by the presence of water in the soil is known as
keyMemory: Water against below-grade wall = hydrostatic pressure.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Hydrostatic pressure
Ulit: hydrostatic pressure.
Kapag tubig sa lupa ang pinanggagalingan ng pressure laban sa basement wall o below-grade portion, ang tawag diyan ay hydrostatic pressure. Hindi soil bearing pressure, dahil ang soil bearing pressure ay support capacity issue sa ilalim ng footing. (Quizlet)
Elimination method:
Keyword mo dito ay presence of water in the soil.
Water pressure = hydrostatic.
Kapag load from structure onto soil = bearing pressure.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Water against below-grade wall = hydrostatic pressure.

question: A geological or ground condition considered in determining the size and type of foundation of the building
keyMemory: Sa ALE, “foundation size and type” often points to soil bearing pressure.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Soil bearing pressure
Ulit: soil bearing pressure.
Ito ang pangunahing determinant sa sizing at choice ng foundation sa ganitong reviewer item. Siyempre sa real life maraming factors, pero sa ALE-style question, ang pinaka-direct answer ay soil bearing pressure dahil ito ang direktang basis kung gaano kalaki o anong type ng footing/foundation ang kailangan. (Quizlet)
Bakit mali ang iba:
Soil chemistry may matter for corrosion or sulfate attack, pero hindi iyon ang first answer dito.
Particle size distribution useful sa soil characterization, pero hindi iyon ang direct sizing parameter na gustong hanapin.
Water level important din, pero again, not the best answer sa wording.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE, “foundation size and type” often points to soil bearing pressure.

question: What method is being done on the earth’s surface to compress the soil prior to concreting?
keyMemory: Tamping = pag-compact ng soil.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Soil Tamping
Ulit: soil tamping.
Very direct ito. Ang action na pang-compress ng soil ay tamping.
Huwag kang malito sa tempering — ibang word iyon at hindi iyon ang compaction action na hinahanap dito. Dewatering is removal of water, soil poisoning is termite treatment. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Compress soil = compact.
Sa choices, alin ang closest to compaction? Tamping.
Simple pero common trap ang similarity ng “tempering” at “tamping.”
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tamping = pag-compact ng soil.

question: What is the disadvantage of using steel scaffolding over wood scaffolding?
keyMemory: Steel scaffold = stronger, more durable, but mas mahal.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. More expensive
Ulit: more expensive.
Steel scaffolding is usually stronger, more durable, and mas reliable sa heavy loads kaysa wood. Kaya ang pinaka-obvious disadvantage niya sa choices ay higher cost. Yung “can only support light loadings” kabaligtaran pa nga ng usual advantage ng steel. (APAC Scaffold)
Elimination method:
Tanggalin agad ang light loadings kasi steel is known for higher capacity.
“Takes longer time to assemble” hindi ito ang most universal disadvantage.
“Requires more space” hindi rin main issue.
So pinaka-malinaw: more expensive.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Steel scaffold = stronger, more durable, but mas mahal.

question: The soil material not recommended for foundation-bed because of the danger of flowing or moving is:
keyMemory: Sa “flowing or moving soil” questions, red-flag answer madalas ang quicksand.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. quicksand
Ulit: quicksand.
Ito halos kapareho ng Page 1 logic sa weak or unstable soil. Sa choices, ang pinaka-clear na may danger of flowing or shifting ay quicksand dahil liquefied or unstable saturated granular condition iyan. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kung may choice na sobrang unstable at literal na kilalang moving soil condition, iyon ang sagot.
Kahit hindi mo kabisado lahat, quicksand stands out as the worst.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa “flowing or moving soil” questions, red-flag answer madalas ang quicksand.

question: Temporary supports designed to carry forms for beams and slabs
keyMemory: Forms for beams/slabs need shoring.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Shoring
Ulit: shoring.
Ang shoring ang temporary support system para dalhin ang load ng formwork, especially for beams and slabs, habang hindi pa kaya ng concrete ang sarili niyang load. Ito ang standard construction term. (AnkiMaker)
Bakit mali ang iba:
Brace gives lateral stability, but not the main term here.
Staking pang-layout.
Pinning hindi ito ang standard answer sa ganitong definition.

Forms for beams/slabs need shoring.

question: A temporary framework for supporting a structure under construction that is not yet capable of supporting itself.
keyMemory: Falsework supports the unfinished structure. Scaffold supports the workers.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Falsework
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Falsework.
Bakit falsework? Kasi iyan mismo ang technical term para sa temporary support ng structure habang hindi pa nito kaya ang sarili niyang load. Isipin mo beam, arch, slab, o form-supported member na hindi pa cured o hindi pa complete. Ang keyword dito ay supporting a structure. Hindi workers ang sinusuportahan, hindi access platform ang point ng tanong, kundi yung mismong structural member habang under construction pa. (ARCHISPEAKING)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Scaffold is for workers, tools, access, at minsan materials, pero hindi iyan ang tamang term para sa temporary support ng unfinished structural member.
Stagework hindi ito ang standard term na hinahanap dito.
Framework masyadong broad. Totoo na falsework is a kind of framework, pero ang ALE gusto rito ay ang exact construction term. (ARCHISPEAKING)
Elimination method:
Kapag may tanong na temporary support of the structure itself, piliin mo ang falsework.
Kapag temporary platform for workers, iyon ang scaffold.
Iyan ang classic board trap: structure support versus worker support.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Falsework supports the unfinished structure. Scaffold supports the workers.

question: For a Class A concrete mix, how many bags of cement are needed for a 2 cubic volume?
keyMemory: Sa ALE reviewer logic: Class A, 1 cu.m = 9 bags, kaya 2 cu.m = 18 bags.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 18 bags
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 18 bags.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat: sa reviewer/ALE table, Class A mix for 1 cubic meter commonly uses 9 bags of cement when the table is based on 40-kg bags. So kung 2 cubic meters, then simple lang:
9 bags × 2 = 18 bags. (Studocu)
Bakit hindi 10, 12, o 20?
Kasi once kabisado mo na ang memory peg na Class A = 9 bags/cu.m., automatic mali na ang 10, 12, at 20 para sa 2 cubic meters.
Common trap ito kapag hindi kabisado ang estimating table. Doon ka nililigaw ng choices. (Studocu)
Elimination method:
Una, tandaan mo ang reviewer memory peg: Class A = 9 bags per cubic meter.
Pangalawa, kapag 2 cubic meters, doblehin lang.
Hindi mo na kailangang manghula.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE reviewer logic: Class A, 1 cu.m = 9 bags, kaya 2 cu.m = 18 bags.

question: Concrete proportions for “Class B” is:
keyMemory: Class B = 1 : 2.5 : 5.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 1 : 2.5 : 5
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 1 : 2.5 : 5.
Makikita mo rin mismo sa printed note sa page mo:
Class B = 1 : 2.5 : 5.
Ito iyong ginagamit sa reviewer para sa slab on fill at non-bearing walls. So dito, direct memory question talaga ito. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba?
1:2:4 is Class A, hindi B.
1:3:6 is Class C.
1:3.5:6 is the distractor na mukhang believable pero hindi standard reviewer answer dito. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kapag may mix proportion question, huwag mo lang i-memorize nang paisa-isa. I-chain mo:
AA = 1:1.5:3
A = 1:2:4
B = 1:2.5:5
C = 1:3:6
Kapag ganyan mo siya iniisip, mas mabilis ang elimination.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Class B = 1 : 2.5 : 5.

question: Standard concrete mix for beams, girders, slabs, stairs and columns is:
keyMemory: Beams, girders, slabs, stairs, columns = Class A.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. “A”
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Class A.
Bakit Class A? Kasi structural members ito: beams, girders, slabs, stairs, columns. Sa reviewer table, Class A = 1:2:4 at iyan ang standard mix para sa ordinary structural reinforced concrete members. Hindi na ito slab on fill lang. Hindi na rin ito mas special mix gaya ng underwater or retaining wall condition. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba?
AA is richer mix, usually for more demanding situations in the reviewer note.
B is for lighter/non-bearing applications.
AAA hindi iyon standard class na ginagamit dito sa choices. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kapag structural RC members ang binanggit, malakas ang sagot na Class A.
Kapag slab on fill at non-bearing wall, doon mo iisipin si Class B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Beams, girders, slabs, stairs, columns = Class A.

question: The water cement ratio in concrete is?

choices.text: The ratio of the weight of water to the volume of cement
The ratio of the volume of water to the volume of cement
The ratio of the weight of water to the weight of cement
The ratio of the volume of water to the weight of cement

answer: c. The ratio of the weight of water to the weight of cement
keyMemory: Water-cement ratio is by weight, not by volume.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. The ratio of the weight of water to the weight of cement
Ulit: weight of water to weight of cement.
Ito importante. Hindi volume ang base nito sa tamang definition. Water-cement ratio is by weight or mass, not by volume. Kaya choice c ang tama. Ito ang classic definition sa concrete technology. (Wikipedia)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Ang a, b, at d lahat may problem na volume ang ginamit sa isa o parehong side. Iyan ang trap. Kapag nakakita ka ng water-cement ratio question, bantayan mo agad kung weight ba o volume.
Sa ALE, malaking clue ang salitang ratio pero mas mahalagang clue ang tamang basis of measurement. (Wikipedia)
Elimination method:
Kapag w/c ratio, isipin mo agad:
w = weight of water
c = weight of cement
Simple. Huwag papaloko sa volume choices.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Water-cement ratio is by weight, not by volume.

question: Mixing of concrete by this method is usually used because it is generally less expensive:
keyMemory: Cheaper does not always mean better. Sa tanong na ito, cheaper = hand mixing.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. hand mixing
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay hand mixing.
Bakit hand mixing? Kasi sa small works, short runs, at simpleng site operations, hand mixing is the cheaper option dahil wala kang mixer rental, fuel, power, o plant setup. Hindi ibig sabihin mas maganda siya sa quality, pero ang tanong ay less expensive, hindi best quality. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Machine mixing at batching plant mixing generally give better consistency, pero mas mahal ang equipment at setup.
All of the above mali dahil hindi puwedeng sabay-sabay silang “generally less expensive.” (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Watch the phrase less expensive.
Kapag cost lang ang usapan at hindi quality, speed, or scale, doon pumapasok si hand mixing.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Cheaper does not always mean better. Sa tanong na ito, cheaper = hand mixing.

question: What is the correct sequence when mixing is done manually with shovels?
keyMemory: Sa hand mixing: sand + cement first, then gravel, then water.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Sand – Cement – Gravel – Water
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Sand – Cement – Gravel – Water.
Kapag hand mixing, standard reviewer logic is:
Una, dry mix muna ang sand at cement para maging uniform ang binder.
Pangalawa, saka isasama ang gravel.
Pangatlo, saka mo idadagdag ang water.
Kaya choice c ang natitira. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba?
a mali kasi bakit mauuna ang gravel kaysa cement-sand blending.
b mali dahil nilagay ang water bago gravel, which is poor sequence sa hand mixing logic ng reviewer.
d mali dahil mas ideal i-blend muna ang sand at cement before the coarse aggregate. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Manual mixing = isipin mo dry mix first.
At sa dry mix, sand + cement muna.
After uniform color, saka gravel.
Then water.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa hand mixing: sand + cement first, then gravel, then water.

question: What is the correct sequence when mixing is done in a one bagger mixer?
keyMemory: Manual = sand, cement, gravel, water. One-bagger = gravel, sand, cement, water.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Gravel – Sand – Cement – Water
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Gravel – Sand – Cement – Water.
Ito ang reviewer logic para sa one-bagger mixer: inuuna ang aggregates, then cement, then water. Sa general mixing guidance, common din ang idea na coarse and fine aggregates first, followed by cementitious materials, tapos water para ma-achieve ang tamang consistency. (NPCA)
Bakit hindi c gaya ng hand mixing?
Kasi magkaiba ang logic ng manual mixing at mixer sequence sa reviewer.
Sa hand mixing, gusto mong i-dry blend ang sand at cement muna.
Sa one-bagger, the reviewer expects gravel – sand – cement – water.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b may water bago gravel, unlikely.
c hand-mixing logic iyan, hindi one-bagger logic.
d hindi ito ang standard sequence na gusto ng reviewer item. (NPCA)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Manual = sand, cement, gravel, water.
One-bagger = gravel, sand, cement, water.

question: The addition of water to make the concrete mix attain its plastic consistency is called:
keyMemory: Sa reviewer wording na ito, regaging ang expected answer. Pero tandaan mo rin na sa real literature, madalas lumalabas ang retempering as a closely related term.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Regaging
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Regaging.
Ito iyong medyo old-school reviewer term. Sa wording na “addition of water to make the concrete mix attain its plastic consistency,” ang expected reviewer answer ay regaging. May mga sources na halos ginagamit na magkalapit ang regaging at retempering, kaya ito iyong type ng question na puwedeng magulo sa actual construction literature. Pero sa reviewer phrasing na ganito, ang hinahanap na sagot ay regaging. (Scribd)
Bakit hindi retempering?
Sa mas common modern usage, retempering is adding water and remixing concrete that has started to stiffen or lose workability. Kaya puwedeng malito ka. Pero dahil ang question ay naka-frame as a reviewer-definition item, regaging ang mas expected board-style answer dito. (Scribd)
Elimination method:
Kapag old reviewer vocabulary ang dating, huwag agad sumandal sa mas modern term na mas familiar sa’yo.
Basahin ang phrasing.
Board strategy dito: choose the term the reviewer is likely using, which is regaging.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer wording na ito, regaging ang expected answer.
Pero tandaan mo rin na sa real literature, madalas lumalabas ang retempering as a closely related term.

question: The maximum slump or consistency of concrete for heavy mass construction is:
keyMemory: Heavy mass construction = lower slump, max 75 mm sa reviewer table.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 75 mm (3”)
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 75 mm or 3 inches.
Sa reviewer table for slump requirements, ang heavy mass construction has a lower allowable slump because hindi mo kailangan ng sobrang fluid na mix doon. Kaya ang maximum na inilalagay sa ganitong reviewer is 75 mm (3”). (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Ang 100, 125, at 150 mm masyado nang mataas para sa heavy mass construction sa reviewer standard na ito.
Kapag sobrang taas ng slump, mas fluid ang mix — hindi iyan ang typical target sa heavy mass work.
Elimination method:
Keyword: heavy mass construction.
Usually mas controlled at mas mababa ang slump kaysa sa more congested or thinner reinforced members.
So hanapin mo ang mas mababang realistic value. That leaves 75 mm.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Heavy mass construction = lower slump, max 75 mm sa reviewer table.

question: The complete records of test conduction (slump, compression test, etc.) shall be preserved and made available for inspection during the progress of construction and after completion of the projects for a period of not less than

Paano mag-eliminate?
Kung hindi mo kabisado, tingnan mo ang pattern ng reviewer-type retention periods. Ang 1 year at 2 years madalas mukhang bitin para sa construction records. Ang 4 years puwedeng mukhang OA sa old reviewer wording. Kaya ang natitirang pinaka-board-friendly answer ay 3 years.

Sa ALE reviewer logic, test records = keep for at least 3 years.

question: Who will shoulder the cost of the retrofitting of a structural column if the compressive testing of the ready mixed concrete failed to meet its minimum strength?

choices.text: Structural Engineer who approved the concrete mixing and placing methodology
Site Architect who oversaw the concrete pouring of the structural column

keyMemory: Kapag reviewer note mismo ang nagsabi, iyon ang pinakamalakas na clue. Dito: ready-mix supplier ang sasagot.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Ready Mix concrete supplier
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Ready Mix concrete supplier.
Dito, may malaking clue tayo kasi nasa printed note mismo sa page mo ang sagot. Nakalagay doon na kapag bumagsak ang 28th-day testing, then pati backup specimen bumagsak din, at pati core test hindi pumasa, then retrofitting method must be employed, and “retrofitting and coring cost will be shouldered by the ready-mix supplier.”
Ibig sabihin, hindi ito guesswork. Nasa note na mismo sa reviewer page. Kaya sa board strategy, kapag may explanatory note sa baba ng tanong, gamitin mo iyon. Huwag mong balewalain.
Bakit mali ang iba?
General contractor hindi siya ang direct supplier ng failed ready-mix product.
Structural engineer hindi siya ang producer ng concrete.
Site architect lalo na, hindi siya ang accountable sa material strength failure ng supplied concrete.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang problem ay material strength failure ng ready-mix, isipin mo agad: sino ang may direct responsibility sa supplied concrete?
Sagot: ready-mix supplier.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag reviewer note mismo ang nagsabi, iyon ang pinakamalakas na clue. Dito: ready-mix supplier ang sasagot.

question: The manufacturers’ specifications do not permit the use of concrete that has been allowed to stand for more than:
keyMemory: Para sa ALE reviewer answer, tandaan mo: 1.5 hours. Pero sa updated ASTM, hindi na iyan laging automatic default.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. 1-1/2 hours
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 1-1/2 hours.
Ito ang classic old-rule answer: 90 minutes or 1.5 hours. Iyan ang matagal na traditional rule sa ready-mixed concrete practice, kaya sa old ALE reviewer logic, ito ang tamang sagot. (LinkedIn)
Pero ito ang importanteng nuance:
Sa current ASTM C94 since 2021, hindi na automatic na fixed 90-minute rule ang default. Ang discharge time is now supposed to be set by the purchaser/project specification. So sa totoong updated standards, mas nuanced na ito. Pero dahil reviewer-style item ang hawak natin at ganyang choices ang binigay, ang expected answer ay 1-1/2 hours. (LinkedIn)

question: When chutes are use in transporting concrete, the slope of the chute must not exceed:
keyMemory: Sa chute slope: max = 1V : 2H min = 1V : 3H
explanation:
Correct answer: a. 1 vertical to 2 horizontal
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 1 vertical to 2 horizontal.
Sa standard concrete placing guidance, ang chute slope should be not greater than 1 vertical to 2 horizontal and not smaller than 1 vertical to 3 horizontal. Ibig sabihin, acceptable range siya, pero kapag tinanong kang must not exceed, ang upper limit ang hahanapin mo: 1:2.
Bakit mali ang iba?
3:1 sobrang tarik, delikado sa segregation.
1:1 masyadong matarik din.
3:3 equals 1:1, mali rin.
Kaya kahit hindi mo kabisado ang exact range, puwede mong alisin ang mga mukhang sobrang steep.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa chute slope:
max = 1V : 2H
min = 1V : 3H

Correct answer: d. poured on the same day
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay poured on the same day.
Sa ordinary reinforced concrete beam-and-slab framing, ang usual logic ay monolithic pouring para mag-behave silang integrated at maiwasan ang unnecessary cold joints. Kaya sa reviewer answer, same day ang tamang sagot. (Jonathan Ochshorn)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Beam only o slab only sounds too isolated as a default answer.
One day apart possible lang iyan kung may designed construction joint at approved procedure, pero hindi iyan ang normal reviewer default.
Sa board exam, ang hinahanap ay ang general standard practice, hindi special exception.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Beam and slab are usually poured monolithically — kaya same day.

question: First thing to do before concrete pouring of a RC column (2nd lift)

choices.text: Provide cement grout or structural epoxy
Provide windows on 2 sides of the column form

answer: b. Provide cement grout or structural epoxy
keyMemory: Kapag may 2nd lift, isipin mo agad: old concrete to new concrete bond.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Provide cement grout or structural epoxy
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay provide cement grout or structural epoxy.
Ang clue dito ay “RC column (2nd lift)”. Ibig sabihin, may existing hardened concrete ka na sa lower lift, tapos magpapatuloy ka ng bagong pour. So ang issue na hinaharap mo ay construction joint / bonding between old and new concrete. Kaya ang pinaka-logical at reviewer-expected answer ay cement grout or structural epoxy bilang bonding treatment. (ScienceDirect)
Bakit hindi windows?
Ang windows sa formwork ay para sa inspection, placement, at vibration lalo na sa tall forms. Useful iyan, pero hindi iyan ang pinakaunang sagot kapag ang clue ay 2nd lift. Ang 2nd-lift clue points to joint treatment, not just form access. (Gharpedia)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may 2nd lift, isipin mo agad: old concrete to new concrete bond.

question: If concrete remains in a transit mixer longer than is recommended by ASTM C94, what should be done?

choices.text: Five gallons [19 L] of water should be added to the concrete.
A plasticizer should be added to the concrete.
Air should be entrained in the concrete.

answer: d. The concrete should be rejected
keyMemory: Kapag lumampas sa permitted ASTM-style discharge limit, reviewer answer = reject.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. The concrete should be rejected
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay The concrete should be rejected.
Sa old ASTM C94 reviewer logic, kapag lumampas sa permitted discharge time at wala kang approved basis to extend, the safe exam answer is reject the load. Hindi basta magdadagdag ng tubig, hindi rin basta-basta magpa-plasticizer o mag-e-entrain ng air para iligtas ang batch sa exam setting. (LinkedIn)
Ito ang common trap:
May choices na parang “solution-oriented” — dagdag tubig, dagdag admixture. Pero sa board exam, kapag may clear exceedance sa permitted limit, the conservative answer is reject. Lalo na dahil ang dagdag-tubig ay puwedeng sumira sa water-cement ratio. (LinkedIn)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag lumampas sa permitted ASTM-style discharge limit, reviewer answer = reject.

question: Truck-mixed concrete governed by ASTM C94 is delivered to a job site for use in a reinforced concrete slab. The concrete should be delivered and discharged within what time limit after the introduction of water?
keyMemory: Sa board-review answer, ASTM C94 = 90 minutes. Sa updated actual standard, hindi na laging automatic 90 minutes.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 90 min
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 90 minutes.
Ito ang gusto kong idiin sa’yo kasi pwedeng malito ka rito:
For ALE reviewer / older ASTM C94 logic, the answer is 90 minutes. Iyan ang classic traditional rule na matagal na ginagamit sa reviewers at exams. (LinkedIn)
Pero ito ang updated reality:
ASTM C94 was revised in 2021, and the standard no longer automatically fixes a universal 90-minute discharge rule. Instead, the purchaser sets the discharge time limit, or the producer states it if the purchaser does not. So kung actual updated-standard question ito sa real-world specification review, kailangan mong basahin ang project specs. Pero dahil ang tanong mo rito ay old-style reviewer item with classic choices, 90 min pa rin ang dapat mong piliin sa ALE context. (LinkedIn)
Elimination method:
Huwag kang magpapa-distract sa 30 at 60 dahil masyado silang maikli para sa classic ASTM C94 reviewer question.
120 naman usually lumalabas lang bilang extension or other modified condition, hindi iyong classic base answer.
Kaya natitira ang 90.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa board-review answer, ASTM C94 = 90 minutes.
Sa updated actual standard, hindi na laging automatic 90 minutes.


Sige, Page 5 tayo. Tuloy natin nang maayos, in order, at mentor-style pa rin.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Concrete vibrator = motor + flexible hose/shaft + pang-compact ng fresh concrete.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Concrete vibrator
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Concrete vibrator.
Bakit? Kasi ang nasa larawan ay mukhang motor unit na may flexible hose at vibrating head sa dulo. Iyan ang classic na itsura ng concrete vibrator. Ginagamit ito para ma-compact ang fresh concrete at mailabas ang trapped air.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag may mahaba at flexible na hose na nakakabit sa motor, tapos ginagamit sa concreting, huwag mo isipin agad water hose o pump. Madalas iyan ay vibrator.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Water pump usually may inlet/outlet setup at pangbomba ng tubig ang form.
Mixer dapat drum-type o mixing chamber ang itsura.
Water hose hose lang iyon, wala dapat motor assembly na ganito.
Elimination method:
Tanggalin mo agad ang mixer kasi wala kang drum.
Tanggalin ang water hose kasi may machine body.
Pagpilian mo na lang pump at vibrator, then tingnan mo ang flexible shaft/hose — doon mo makikilala si concrete vibrator.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Concrete vibrator = motor + flexible hose/shaft + pang-compact ng fresh concrete.

question: What is the usual time for concrete to initially set after mixing?
keyMemory: Sa ALE reviewer style, initial set = around 30 to 120 minutes.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. 30–120 minutes
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 30 to 120 minutes.
Board-review logic ito. Ang initial setting time ng concrete pagkatapos mahalo ay hindi sobrang bilis na parang 1 to 15 minutes, at hindi rin usually inaabot agad ng 3 hours as the standard “usual” answer sa ganitong reviewer. Kaya ang pinaka-reasonable at expected answer ay 30–120 minutes.
Bakit mali ang iba?
15–60 minutes puwedeng masyadong maaga for usual full initial-set range.
60–180 minutes puwedeng masyadong delayed para sa “usual” reviewer answer.
1–15 minutes obvious na sobrang bilis.
Elimination method:
Kapag “usual” ang tanong, hanapin ang middle realistic range.
Tanggalin ang sobrang maiksi at sobrang mahaba.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE reviewer style, initial set = around 30 to 120 minutes.

question: The hardening of concrete is called:
keyMemory: Setting = hardening/stiffening phase. Curing = moisture care para lumakas ang concrete.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. setting
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay setting.
Dito kailangan malinaw ang difference ng terms.
Setting is the stiffening or hardening action of fresh cement paste/concrete after mixing.
Curing is the process of keeping concrete moist and in proper condition so it gains strength well.
Kaya kung ang tanong ay “The hardening of concrete is called”, ang expected answer sa reviewer wording ay setting.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Caulking sealing of joints iyon.
Drying hindi iyan ang tamang technical process na gusto ng tanong.
Curing important siya, pero treatment/process iyon para sa proper strength development, hindi simpleng tawag sa hardening itself.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa ano ang tawag sa pag-stiffen o pag-set ng fresh concrete, sagot mo ay setting.
Kapag ang tanong ay paano ito inaalagaan para lumakas, sagot mo ay curing.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Setting = hardening/stiffening phase.
Curing = moisture care para lumakas ang concrete.

question: This is a rather slow process in which the cement and water unite to form compounds that give strength and durability to the concrete.
keyMemory: Concrete gains strength mainly through hydration, not just drying.
explanation:
Correct answer: Hydration
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hydration.
Mukhang fill-in or definition-type item ito kahit walang visible choices.
Ang process na binabanggit dito ay hydration — chemical reaction ng cement + water na siyang bumubuo ng compounds responsible for strength and durability.
Ito ang clue:
Kapag sinabi ng tanong na cement and water unite chemically to form strength-giving compounds, hindi iyan simpleng drying. Iyan ay hydration.
Common trap:
Maraming nag-iisip na tumitigas ang concrete dahil “natutuyo.” Hindi.
Ang totoong pangunahing dahilan ay chemical reaction, hindi simpleng evaporation.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Concrete gains strength mainly through hydration, not just drying.

question: To allow concrete to dry slowly by keeping it moist to attain maximum strength
keyMemory: Curing = keeping concrete moist so strength can properly develop.

Hardening result iyon, hindi maintenance process.
Setting earlier stage ng stiffening.
Tempering hindi iyan ang tamang term dito.

Automatic iyon sa curing.

Correct answer: b. stirrups
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Stirrups.
Sa beam, ang transverse bars na humahawak at nagko-confine sa main longitudinal reinforcement ay stirrups. Sila rin ang tumutulong sa shear resistance. Kaya sila ang tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Tie bar mas generic at hindi ito ang standard term dito.
Lateral ties mas common sa columns.
Tie wires pang-tali ng rebars during assembly, pero hindi sila ang rebar member na tinutukoy ng tanong.
Elimination method:
Keyword: on a beam.
Kapag beam, ang transverse holding rebar = stirrups.
Kapag column, doon mo mas maiisip ang ties.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Beam = stirrups.
Column = ties.

Sige, Page 6 tayo. Dahan-dahan lang, one by one, at board-exam reasoning style.

question: The splice for the top bar of a RC beam is
keyMemory: Top bar splice = midspan. Bottom bar splice = near support.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. located at the midspan
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay located at the midspan.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat: sa beam detailing, hindi ka basta naglalagay ng splice kung saan pinakamalaki ang stress ng bar. Ang tanong dito ay top bar. Sa ordinary reviewer logic, ang top bars are usually spliced at midspan, habang ang bottom bars mas madalas near support.
Bakit? Kasi sa board-review framing, ang memory rule ay:
•	Top bars splice at midspan
•	Bottom bars splice near supports
Oo, sa tunay na structural behavior, puwedeng mag-iba ang tension zones depende kung simply supported o continuous beam. Pero sa ALE reviewer type na ganitong maikli ang tanong, ang expected answer usually is the standard detailing memory rule.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. near the support — ito ang mas iniuugnay sa bottom bars sa reviewer shortcut.
c. anywhere — automatic mali. Sa structural detailing, hindi “kahit saan.”
d. near the bottom chord — hindi malinaw at hindi tamang splice-location answer.
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang tanong na top bar splice, hanapin mo agad ang paired memory:
top bar = midspan
bottom bar = near support
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan malilito ka sa actual structural analysis ng beam behavior. Pero kung reviewer-style memory item ang format, sundin mo muna ang standard exam rule.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Top bar splice = midspan.
Bottom bar splice = near support.

question: The stressing of unbounded tendons after concrete has cured is
keyMemory: After curing = post-tensioning. Iyan ang mabilis na exam shortcut.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Post-tensioning
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Post-tensioning.
Napaka-keyword-based nito. Ang clue ay “after concrete has cured.”
Kapag ang tendon ay ini-stress after tumigas o nag-cure na ang concrete, ang tawag doon ay post-tensioning.
Bakit hindi pre-tensioning?
Sa pre-tensioning, inuunang i-stress ang steel bago i-cast o bago mag-set ang concrete.
So board trick ito: pre versus post.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Pre-casting ay method ng paggawa ng element, hindi stressing method.
Lift slab ay construction method, hindi tendon stressing system.
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang trap word na after.
After curing = post.
Before concreting/casting = pre.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
After curing = post-tensioning.
Iyan ang mabilis na exam shortcut.

question: Free fall or drop of concrete for unexposed work should not exceed:
keyMemory: Maximum free fall of fresh concrete: huwag sobra para iwas segregation. Reviewer answer dito: 5 feet.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 5 feet
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 5 feet.
Ito ay tungkol sa pag-iwas sa segregation. Kapag masyadong mataas ang bagsak ng fresh concrete, naghihiwalay ang coarse aggregate, mortar, at paste. Kaya may recommended maximum drop.
Sa reviewer logic, ang sagot dito ay 5 feet.
Bakit hindi 8 or 9 feet?
Masyadong mataas iyon. Mas mataas ang drop, mas mataas ang chance ng segregation.
Bakit hindi 3 feet?
Puwede iyang magmukhang conservative, pero ang standard reviewer answer dito ay 5 feet.
Elimination method:
Kapag free fall ng concrete ang tanong, alisin mo ang mga sobrang taas na choices.
Tapos pagpilian mo ang realistic values. Sa ganitong item, 5 feet ang common answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Maximum free fall of fresh concrete: huwag sobra para iwas segregation.
Reviewer answer dito: 5 feet.

question: The cause of cracks in concrete due to hardening and contraction from temperature changes is due to:

choices.text: exposure to intense heat from the sun

keyMemory: Kapag usapan ay hardening + contraction + cracking, isipin mo agad shrinkage.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. shrinkage of concrete
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay shrinkage of concrete.
Ito ang pinakaimportanteng clue:
“hardening and contraction from temperature changes”
Iyan mismo ang description ng shrinkage-related cracking.
Bakit shrinkage?
Habang tumitigas ang concrete at nawawalan ng moisture or nag-aadjust sa temperature, kumukontra siya. Kapag na-restrain ang movement na iyon, nagkaka-cracks.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. intense heat from the sun — puwedeng contributing factor, pero hindi iyan ang pinaka-technical answer na hinahanap.
c. lack of proper moisture — puwede ring mag-contribute sa drying shrinkage, pero mas umbrella term pa rin ang shrinkage mismo.
d. vibration of forms — hindi iyan ang pangunahing cause ng ganitong crack type.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay naghahanap ng technical name ng phenomenon, huwag kang pipili ng simpleng cause lang gaya ng init o lack of moisture kung may mas exact engineering term sa choices.
Common trap sa board exam:
Pipiliin mo ang lack of proper moisture kasi totoo rin naman. Pero mas exact at mas central na sagot ay shrinkage.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag usapan ay hardening + contraction + cracking, isipin mo agad shrinkage.

question: Large cracks in long, continuous length of concrete can be prevented by inserting or placing reinforcement and by placing:
keyMemory: Sa long concrete runs, contraction joints control cracking.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. contraction joints
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay contraction joints.
Ito ang logic: sa mahahabang continuous concrete placements, gusto mong kontrolin kung saan puputok o mag-crack ang concrete. Hindi mo laging mapipigilan ang cracking, pero puwede mo siyang i-control. Iyan ang trabaho ng contraction joints, also called control joints in many contexts.
Bakit hindi wire mesh?
Kasi sinabi na ng tanong na “by inserting or placing reinforcement”. So hiwalay na idea na ang hinahanap pagkatapos noon.
Bakit hindi construction joints?
Construction joints are stopping points in pouring sequence, hindi sila primarily for crack control.
Bakit hindi shrinkage joints?
Sa standard terminology, ang mas accepted answer dito ay contraction joints.
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang purpose:
•	Para ba sa stopping concrete work? construction joint iyon.
•	Para ba sa allowing expansion/movement? expansion joint iyon.
•	Para ba sa controlling shrinkage cracking? contraction joint iyon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa long concrete runs, contraction joints control cracking.

question: A joint where two successive placement of concrete meet is called
keyMemory: General meeting of successive pours = construction joint. If already hardened before next batch = cold joint.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Construction Joint
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Construction Joint.
Ito ang general term kapag dalawang magkasunod na placement ng concrete ang nagtatagpo. Ibig sabihin, may unang pour, tumigil, tapos may kasunod na pour — ang meeting line nila ay construction joint.
Bakit hindi cold joint?
Diyan madalas nadadali ang examinee.
Ang cold joint is a more specific condition: nangyari iyon kapag tumigas na ang naunang concrete bago naidikit ang susunod.
Pero ang mas general at planned term para sa meeting of successive placements ay construction joint.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Control joint para sa crack control.
Expansion joint para sa movement allowance.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay general lang na successive placement meet, answer first with construction joint.
Kapag sinabi niyang tumigas na muna ang unang surface bago naidagdag ang kasunod, saka ka pupunta sa cold joint.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
General meeting of successive pours = construction joint.
If already hardened before next batch = cold joint.

question: The vertical and horizontal surface in reinforced concrete where concreting was stopped and continued later.
keyMemory: Kapag pouring schedule ang issue, madalas construction joint ang sagot.

Correct answer: d. Construction Joint

Halos kapareho ito ng previous question.
Ang clue dito ay “where concreting was stopped and continued later.”
That is exactly the language of a construction joint.
Bakit hindi cold joint?
Again, cold joint is not just “stopped and continued later.”
Mas specific iyon sa condition na na-harden na muna ang unang concrete before the next batch was placed.
Elimination method:
Keyword watch:
•	Stopped and continued later = construction joint
•	Hardened before next batch = cold joint
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag pouring schedule ang issue, madalas construction joint ang sagot.

question: A separation of either vertical or concrete pours designed to defer movement in case of wind loading or other loads so as not to compromise the integrity of the concrete structure itself. It is usually joined together by non-metallic strips to hold the two slabs in place; at the same time giving flex when there is movement
keyMemory: Movement between structural parts = expansion joint.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Expansion Joint
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Expansion Joint.
Dito ang pinaka-keywords ay:
•	defer / allow movement
•	hindi ma-compromise ang structure
•	may flex when there is movement
Iyan ang signature ng expansion joint or movement joint.
Bakit hindi control joint?
Control joint is mainly to control shrinkage cracking in predetermined lines.
Bakit hindi construction or cold joint?
Wala silang pangunahing purpose na mag-accommodate ng movement between parts.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang emphasis ay movement allowance, matic ka na dapat sa expansion joint.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Movement between structural parts = expansion joint.

question: Due to temperature effects in materials, concrete walls, slabs of long buildings, new buildings adjoining existing buildings should be provided with
keyMemory: Long walls, slabs, adjoining buildings = think expansion joint.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Expansion joint
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Expansion joint.
Kapag mahahabang walls, slabs, o may new building adjoining an old one, kailangan mong payagan ang independent movement due to temperature changes, differential movement, at minor expansion-contraction effects. Kaya expansion joint ang tamang sagot.
Bakit hindi control joint?
Control joint is more for controlled cracking, especially in slabs and masonry.
Pero kapag ang point ay movement between building parts, expansion joint ang mas tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Flashing pang-waterproofing.
Welded joints lalo nang hindi ang sagot sa movement allowance.
Elimination method:
Tanungin mo ang sarili mo:
Crack-control lang ba? Or movement allowance between parts?
Kapag movement between parts, expansion joint.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Long walls, slabs, adjoining buildings = think expansion joint.

question: Joint employed to reduce restraint by accommodating movement of masonry walls are known as
keyMemory: Masonry walls = control joints ang usual reviewer answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Control joint
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Control joint.
Ito ang medyo tricky kasi baka gusto mong piliin ang expansion joint dahil may word na movement. Pero ang tanong ay specifically about masonry walls. Sa masonry, especially concrete masonry, ang usual term na ginagamit para sa joints accommodating movement and reducing restraint is control joint.
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices:
Specific ang tanong sa masonry walls, hindi generic concrete building separation.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Cold joints hindi ito masonry movement joint.
Block out opening/reservation lang iyan.
Expansion joint puwedeng movement joint din sa ibang contexts, pero sa ALE masonry terminology, ang mas expected answer ay control joint.
Common trap sa board exam:
Pag may salitang movement, automatic expansion joint agad.
Pero kailangan mong basahin ang material context — dito masonry wall.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Masonry walls = control joints ang usual reviewer answer.

question: A joint formed when a concrete surface hardens before the next batch of concrete is placed against it
keyMemory: Hardened first before next pour = cold joint.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Cold joints
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Cold joints.
Ito na mismo ang textbook definition ng cold joint.
Dito, hindi na general successive placement lang. Ang mahalagang clue ay “hardens before the next batch is placed.”
Kapag tumigas muna ang first concrete at saka dumating ang next batch, nagkakaroon ng poor bond plane or visible joint line — that is a cold joint.
Bakit hindi construction joint?
Kasi mas specific ang wording dito kaysa sa earlier questions.
Earlier: meeting of successive placements, stopped and continued later = construction joint.
Dito: hardened first before next batch = cold joint.
Elimination method:
Kapag may phrase na “hardens before next batch”, huwag nang mag-isip pa.
Cold joint na agad iyan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Hardened first before next pour = cold joint.

One-fourth = 25%.

1/4 thickness = 25%
