question: The plan detail below illustrates a vertical concrete masonry control joint used in an exterior wall to allow for all of the following EXCEPT
keyMemory: Control joint in masonry = for shrinkage and temperature movement, not primarily for settlement.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Building settlement
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Building settlement.
Ito ang tanong na may word na EXCEPT, kaya dito maraming nadadale.
Ang control joint sa concrete masonry wall is mainly for:
•	shrinkage
•	thermal contraction / expansion effects
•	movement control within the wall
Pero building settlement is a different issue. Iyan ay mas structural/foundation movement concern, hindi primary function ng ordinary masonry control joint.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Actually hindi sila “mali” in the sense na sila ang movement effects na puwedeng i-accommodate ng joint.
Kaya sila ang hindi mo pipiliin dahil EXCEPT ang tanong.
Elimination method:
Kapag may EXCEPT, baliktarin mo ang utak mo.
Huwag hanapin ang tama. Hanapin ang hindi sakop ng function ng detail.
Common trap sa board exam:
Makikita mo ang word na movement, tapos pipili ka ng thermal effect agad. Mali, kasi iyon nga ang sakop ng joint.
Ang hindi sakop dito ay building settlement.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Control joint in masonry = for shrinkage and temperature movement, not primarily for settlement.

question: Most waterproofed type of mortar joints for wall is
keyMemory: Raked = poor against water. Weathered = better water-shedding joint.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Weathered
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Weathered.
Sa mga choices, ang weathered joint ang pinaka-water-shedding ang behavior. Naka-slope ang finish niya para mas madaling umagos ang tubig palabas at hindi mag-stay sa joint.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Raked joint is one of the least weather-resistant dahil may recess na puwedeng kapitan ng tubig.
Struck joint is generally less favorable kaysa weathered depending on slope orientation and workmanship.
Broom finish hindi ito standard mortar-joint type answer dito.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay most waterproof / weather-resistant, alisin mo agad ang mga may recess tulad ng raked.
Then hanapin ang profile na mas mahusay mag-shed ng water — that is weathered.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Raked = poor against water.
Weathered = better water-shedding joint.

question: It is the ability of material to resist repeated bending or twisting?
keyMemory: Elasticity = kakayahang mag-deform and recover under bending/twisting type action.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Elasticity
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Elasticity.
Dito ang clue ay bending or twisting.
Ang property na tumutukoy sa kakayahan ng material na ma-deform under load and then recover or endure such flexing behavior is elasticity.
Bakit hindi toughness?
Toughness is more about absorbing energy and resisting shock or impact before fracture.
Bakit hindi strength?
Too broad. Strength is general capacity to resist loads.
Bakit hindi hardness?
Hardness is resistance to indentation, scratching, or abrasion.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa flexing, bending, twisting, huwag pumili ng hardness.
Kapag tungkol sa impact, doon mo iisipin ang toughness.
Kapag about recovering from deformation or resisting flexing behavior, elasticity ang mas tamang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Elasticity = kakayahang mag-deform and recover under bending/twisting type action.

Sige, Page 7 tayo. Ito medyo halo na ng prestressing, CHB estimating, at plastering/masonry terms, so ang importante rito hindi lang memorization — kundi marunong kang kumilatis ng clue sa wording.

question: In the design of large shopping centers where space is required, intervals of columns can be wider than the ordinary by adopting a structural method of construction called
keyMemory: Kapag ang objective ay mas malalaking open spaces at mas konting columns, malakas ang sagot na post-tensioning.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Post-tensioning
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Post-tensioning.
Bakit post-tensioning?
Kasi ang pinaka-clue dito ay “wider intervals of columns” at “where space is required.” Ibig sabihin, ang hanap ay system na nagbibigay ng longer spans para mas kaunti ang columns at mas open ang space. Iyan ang very common advantage ng post-tensioned concrete sa malls, parking buildings, commercial floors, at malalaking open-plan spaces.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag sinabi ng item na gusto ng designer na mas maluwag ang space at mas malayo ang pagitan ng columns, isipin mo agad ang long-span system. Sa choices, ang pinakatugma diyan ay post-tensioning.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Compressioning hindi ito ang tamang standard construction term dito. Mukhang distractor lang.
Pre-casting puwedeng makatulong sa speed, pero hindi iyan ang direct answer sa tanong tungkol sa wider column spacing.
Pre-tensioning prestressing method din iyan, yes, pero sa building floor systems for wide open spaces, mas common reviewer answer ang post-tensioning dahil mas associated ito sa in-place slab and beam systems for long spans.
Elimination method:
Una, tanggalin mo agad ang compressioning kasi hindi iyan ang usual named method na gusto ng ALE.
Pangalawa, compare mo pre-casting, pre-tensioning, at post-tensioning.
Ang tanong ay hindi tungkol sa fabrication method o plant production. Ang clue ay long span / wider spacing.
So ang pinakamalakas na natitira: post-tensioning.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang objective ay mas malalaking open spaces at mas konting columns, malakas ang sagot na post-tensioning.

question: Which of the following is an advantage of posttensioned concrete over pretensioned and precast concrete?

choices.text: Quality can be verified before installation.
Cured sections can be sawed to desired lengths.

answer: a. Heavy abutments are not required
keyMemory: Pre-tensioning needs heavy abutments. Post-tensioning does not.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Heavy abutments are not required.
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Heavy abutments are not required.
Ito ang medyo subtle na tanong. Ang pinaka-board-safe answer dito ay heavy abutments are not required.
Bakit?
Sa pre-tensioning, kailangan mo ng strong end abutments o stressing beds kasi inuunang i-stress ang tendons before concreting. Sa post-tensioning, ini-stress ang tendons after concrete has gained enough strength, kaya hindi mo kailangan ang ganoong heavy fixed abutment setup gaya sa pre-tensioning beds.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Kasi ito ang pinaka-technical at pinaka-distinctive advantage ng post-tensioning over pretensioned/precast systems sa reviewer context.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Quality can be verified before installation — mas parang advantage ito ng precast members, hindi specifically ng post-tensioning.
c. Construction time may be reduced — puwedeng totoo in some cases, pero masyado itong broad at hindi unique.
d. Cured sections can be sawed to desired lengths — hindi ito ang defining advantage ng post-tensioning; mas associated pa nga ito sa precast/pretensioned production logic.
Elimination method:
Kapag tinanong ka ng “advantage over pretensioned”, isipin mo agad: ano ang wala sa post-tensioning na kailangan sa pretensioning?
Sagot: heavy abutments/stressing bed.
Iyan ang direct comparison clue.
Common trap sa board exam:
Yung “construction time may be reduced” sounds nice and modern, pero madalas broad statement lang iyan. Mas gusto ng exam ang specific technical difference.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Pre-tensioning needs heavy abutments. Post-tensioning does not.

GFRC ibang specialty iyon.
Cast-in-place concrete sobrang generic — hindi iyan ang specific identity ng company sa reviewer context.

question: Which of the following types of concrete construction is reinforced with tensioned high-strength steel-wire strands?
keyMemory: Tensioned high-strength strands = prestressed concrete.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Prestressed
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Prestressed.
Ito diretso. Kapag may phrase na “tensioned high-strength steel-wire strands,” ang tinutukoy niyan ay prestressed concrete.
Bakit?
Kasi ang essence ng prestressing ay nilalagyan ng pre-applied stress ang steel para ma-counter ang tensile effects sa concrete.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Tilt-up is a construction method.
Precast is a production/fabrication method. Hindi lahat ng precast ay prestressed.
Cast-in-place is also a method, not necessarily prestressed.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo kung ang choices ba ay structural system o construction method.
Ang clue ng tanong ay nasa steel strands under tension. So ang system answer ay prestressed.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tensioned high-strength strands = prestressed concrete.

question: How many pieces of 6" CHB is needed for a 20 square meter wall?
keyMemory: CHB per sq.m = 12.5 pcs ang mabilis na ALE shortcut.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. 250
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 250 pieces.
Ito estimating question. Ang standard board-review shortcut ay:
1 square meter of wall = 12.5 CHB
assuming nominal face size of about 0.40 m × 0.20 m.
So:
20 sq.m × 12.5 pcs/sq.m = 250 pcs
Bakit tama ito?
Kasi sa CHB estimating, ang thickness na 4", 5", 6" affects wall thickness, pero hindi nito binabago ang face area count per square meter. Kaya pareho pa rin ang piece count per sq.m kung pareho ang block face dimensions.
Bakit mali ang iba?
300 masyadong mataas.
183 at 201 hindi tugma sa basic 12.5 pcs/sq.m estimate.
Kapag clean wall area lang ang binigay at walang openings o wastage, ang sagot mo ay direct computation lang.
Elimination method:
Memory peg: 12.5 pcs per sq.m
Then multiply by wall area.
Huwag magpa-intimidate sa dami ng choices.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
CHB per sq.m = 12.5 pcs ang mabilis na ALE shortcut.

answerConceptVisual.detail: Reviewer uses 12.5 pcs CHB per square meter.

question: A fence has a height of 3.0m, length of 6.0m, how many pieces of 6" CHB is needed?
keyMemory: Sa CHB estimating, area first, then × 12.5.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 225
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 225 pieces.
Compute natin:
Area = 3.0 × 6.0 = 18 sq.m
Then:
18 × 12.5 = 225 pcs
Ganoon lang kasimple.
Ito ang common trap:
Kapag may dimensions, may tendency ang examinee na ma-overthink at maghanap pa ng ibang factor. Pero kung walang openings at walang wastage stated, wall area lang ang kailangan.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Hindi sila tugma sa direct CHB estimating shortcut.
Elimination method:
Step 1: kuha ng area.
Step 2: multiply by 12.5.
Step 3: piliin ang eksaktong lumabas.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa CHB estimating, area first, then × 12.5.

answerConceptVisual.title: CHB estimate
answerConceptVisual.detail: Area is 18 m²; using 12.5 pcs/m² gives 225 pcs.

question: Compute the no. of CHB needed if the wall spans 6.0 meters long and 2.40 meters high from the floor to the soffit of the beam with a fixed window 0.60 meters wide and 2.0 meters high? Add 5% wastage.
keyMemory: Sa masonry estimate with openings: subtract openings first, then add wastage last.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 173.25
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 173.25.
Dahan-dahan natin:
Wall area:
6.0 × 2.40 = 14.40 sq.m
Window opening area:
0.60 × 2.0 = 1.20 sq.m
Net wall area:
14.40 - 1.20 = 13.20 sq.m
CHB needed before wastage:
13.20 × 12.5 = 165 pcs
Add 5% wastage:
165 × 1.05 = 173.25 pcs
Kaya tamang sagot: 173.25
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag may opening, ibabawas muna ang opening area bago mag-multiply ng CHB count.
Then saka ka lang mag-add ng wastage.
Bakit mali ang iba?
200.75 too high.
150.00 kulang, mukhang hindi naisama nang tama ang wastage or mali ang net area.
174.00 very tempting kasi parang ni-round off. Pero ang exact computed choice sa options ay 173.25.
Elimination method:
1.	Gross wall area
2.	Minus opening area
3.	Multiply by 12.5
4.	Add wastage
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan inuunang i-add ang wastage bago i-minus ang opening. Mali iyon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa masonry estimate with openings: subtract openings first, then add wastage last.

answerConceptVisual.title: CHB with opening + wastage
answerConceptVisual.main: Net wall less window, then add 5% wastage

question: Which is not used to define or layout the actual thickness of a plaster?
keyMemory: Butones and strings define the plaster plane; straight edge checks and levels it.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Straight edge
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Straight edge.
Bakit straight edge?
Kasi sa plastering work, ang ginagamit para mag-set ng thickness guides ay things like butones at guide strings like nylon cross string.
Ang straight edge ay ginagamit more for checking alignment, leveling, and striking off the plaster surface between guides, not for initially defining or laying out the actual thickness.
Ito ang clue sa tanong:
Ang tanong ay define or layout actual thickness.
Hindi lang basta tool na ginagamit sa plastering.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Nylon cross string ginagamit sa pag-establish ng guide plane.
Butones exactly ginagamit as plaster thickness markers/guides.
None of the above mali dahil may isang malinaw na answer.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo kung ang tool ba ay pang-guide setup or pang-check/finish.
Guide setup = butones, strings
Check/strike = straight edge
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Butones and strings define the plaster plane; straight edge checks and levels it.

answerConceptVisual.title: Plaster thickness guide
answerConceptVisual.main: Straight edge is not used to define/layout plaster thickness
answerConceptVisual.detail: Plaster guides are controlled by screeds/grounds/reference points; straight edge is mainly a leveling/checking tool.

question: The appearance of waterborne salts on the face of masonry is called
keyMemory: White salt deposits on masonry = efflorescence.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Efflorescence
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Efflorescence.
Ito ang white, powdery, salt-like deposit na lumalabas sa masonry surface kapag may moisture na nagdadala ng soluble salts papunta sa surface, tapos nag-e-evaporate ang tubig at naiiwan ang salts.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Calcification hindi ito ang standard masonry defect term dito.
Effluence wrong term.
Permeance property iyon relating to passage of vapor/moisture, not the deposit itself.
Elimination method:
Kapag may tanong tungkol sa white salts on masonry, huwag nang maghanap ng mas komplikadong sagot.
Automatic: efflorescence.
Common trap sa board exam:
Nalilito ang iba sa “effluence” dahil parang tunog technical. Pero ang tamang term ay efflorescence.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
White salt deposits on masonry = efflorescence.

question: Efflorescence on masonry walls is caused by water-soluble salts that are present in which of the following? Check the two that apply. A. Mortar B. Sheathing C. Masonry flashing D. Masonry units E. Masonry ties F. Rigid insulation
keyMemory: Efflorescence = moisture + soluble salts + evaporation. Usual salt sources: mortar at masonry units.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. A, D
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Mortar and Masonry Units.
Bakit iyan?
Ang efflorescence ay iyong puti-puting salt deposits na lumalabas sa masonry surface kapag may moisture na dumaan sa material at dinala niya sa surface ang water-soluble salts. Ang usual sources ng salts na iyan ay:
•	mortar
•	masonry units
Kaya A at D ang tama.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Sheathing hindi iyan ang usual salt source para sa efflorescence ng masonry face.
Masonry flashing pang-control ng water, hindi salt source ang usual point nito.
Masonry ties metal ties iyon, hindi iyan ang hinahanap na main source ng soluble salts.
Rigid insulation hindi rin.
Elimination method
Kapag may tanong sa efflorescence, isipin mo agad:
“Saan nanggagaling ang salts?”
Usually sa cement-based materials at sa masonry body mismo.
So ang dapat mong hanapin ay iyong mga material na actual na may mineral salts.
That leaves mortar and masonry units.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Efflorescence = moisture + soluble salts + evaporation.
Usual salt sources: mortar at masonry units.

MASONRY WALL CONSTRUCTION

question: A course of stones placed on top of cornice crowning the walls is called:
keyMemory: Blocking course ang special course sa ibabaw ng cornice crowning the wall.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Blocking Course
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Blocking Course.
Bakit blocking course?
Ito ang masonry/stonework term para sa course na inilalagay on top of cornice bilang crowning or finishing course. Ang tanong ay very specific sa location and function.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Stretcher is orientation ng masonry unit, hindi special crowning course.
Header orientation din iyan, hindi top-of-cornice course.
Heading course means a course composed of headers. Hindi iyon ang hinahanap ng tanong.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa specific named course sa top ng cornice, huwag kang pipili ng generic brick orientation terms tulad ng stretcher or header.
Hanapin mo ang special term. Iyon si blocking course.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Blocking course ang special course sa ibabaw ng cornice crowning the wall.

question: What is the maximum time period that mixed mortar can be used before hydration has a damaging effect on its final strength?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, mixed mortar has a limited use window — huwag patagalin dahil apektado ang strength at workability.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 2 to 3 1/2 hours
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 2 to 3 1/2 hours.
Ito ang reviewer-type answer.
Ang logic dito: ang mortar has a limited workable life. Kapag masyado nang matagal nakatengga, nagsisimula nang makaapekto ang hydration sa final performance at workability.
Bakit hindi iyong mas mahahabang oras?
3 1/2 to 4 1/2 at 4 1/2 to 6 hours are too long for normal mortar use. Masyado nang risky iyon sa board-review logic.
Bakit hindi iyong pinakamaiksi?
1/2 to 1 1/2 hours puwedeng masyadong conservative para sa typical mortar-use window.
Elimination method
Kapag mortar workability time ang tanong:
•	alisin mo ang sobrang habang oras
•	huwag din agad kunin ang sobrang ikling oras
•	hanapin mo ang realistic middle range
Kaya natitira ang 2 to 3 1/2 hours.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, mixed mortar has a limited use window — huwag patagalin dahil apektado ang strength at workability.

answerConceptVisual.title: Mixed mortar time limit
answerConceptVisual.detail: Mixed mortar should be used before hydration affects final strength.

question: When laying bricks in pattern of alternate headers and stretchers in each course, it is called:
keyMemory: Flemish bond = headers and stretchers alternate in the same course.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Flemish Bond
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Flemish Bond.
Ito classic bond-pattern question.
Kapag within each course ay alternate ang headers and stretchers, ang tawag diyan ay Flemish bond.
Bakit hindi English bond?
Sa English bond, alternate courses ang headers at stretchers, hindi alternate units in the same course.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Common bond usually mostly stretchers with occasional header courses.
Herring-bone ibang decorative pattern iyan.
Elimination method
Very important ito:
•	alternate in each course = Flemish
•	alternate by course = English
Ito ang board trap.
“One course” versus “every other course” — basahin mo nang mabuti.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Flemish bond = headers and stretchers alternate in the same course.

explanation:

Mukhang definition-type item ito, at kahit hindi klaro ang choices sa image, ang description mismo ay sapat na.
Ang phrase na alternating headers and stretchers in each course is already the giveaway.
Bakit Flemish ulit?
Kasi iyan mismo ang defining feature niya.
The added phrase “each header being centered above and below a stretcher” further confirms the pattern alignment.
Elimination method
Kapag ang definition ay parang textbook definition na ng Flemish, huwag mo nang i-overthink.

Definition na mismo ng Flemish bond ang tanong.

question: An arrangement of bricks such that each course consists of alternate bricks having their short sides (headers) and long sides (stretchers) facing outwards, with alternate courses being offset.
keyMemory: Kapag mixed headers and stretchers in every course, Flemish lagi ang unang isipin.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Flemish Bond
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Flemish Bond.
Again, same core clue:
•	headers and stretchers both visible in each course
•	alternating pattern
•	offset courses
All of those point to Flemish bond.
Bakit hindi English?
Dahil English bond is not mixed header-and-stretcher in the same course. It alternates entire header course and entire stretcher course.
Elimination method
Kapag sinabi ng tanong na each course consists of alternate bricks with short and long sides outward, automatic Flemish na iyan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag mixed headers and stretchers in every course, Flemish lagi ang unang isipin.

question: Identify the type of brickwork
keyMemory: Kapag figure-recognition, hanapin mo muna kung same-course mixed pattern ba. Kapag oo, Flemish.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Flemish
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Flemish.
Sa drawing, makikita mo na ang pattern ay may alternating headers and stretchers within the same course. Iyan ang visual signature ng Flemish bond.
Bakit hindi English?
Sa English bond, makikita mo ang isang buong course na stretchers, tapos kasunod buong course na headers. Hindi iyan ang nasa figure.
Bakit hindi stretcher bond?
Sa stretcher bond, puro stretchers lang ang kita sa face. Hindi ganoon ang drawing.
Bakit hindi stack?
Sa stack bond, aligned vertically ang joints and more grid-like ang pattern. Hindi rin iyon ang nasa figure.
Elimination method
Sa image-identification:
•	mixed headers + stretchers in one course = Flemish
•	whole course same type = English
•	all stretchers only = Stretcher bond
•	vertical joints aligned = Stack bond
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag figure-recognition, hanapin mo muna kung same-course mixed pattern ba. Kapag oo, Flemish.

question: A course of headers is inserted every five or six courses. A header is always centered on a stretcher and the joints of each course of headers always align.
keyMemory: Common bond = mostly stretcher courses, with a header course every few courses.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Common Bond
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Common Bond.
Ito ang classic description ng Common Bond, also called American bond in many references. Karaniwan, mostly stretchers ang wall, tapos every several courses, may header course para sa tying action.
Bakit hindi English bond?
Sa English bond, regular ang alternation ng entire stretcher and header courses. Hindi lang every five or six courses.
Bakit hindi Flemish?
Sa Flemish, mixed within the same course ang headers and stretchers.
Bakit hindi herring-bone? obvious na hindi.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	every five or six courses = not English, not Flemish
•	occasional header course only = Common bond
Ito ang reason bakit natatanggal ang iba.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Common bond = mostly stretcher courses, with a header course every few courses.

question: What reinforcement is used in glass blocks?
keyMemory: Reinforcement means pampalakas, hindi pang-seal o pang-bond lang. Sa choices na ito, rebars ang tama.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. rebars
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay rebars.
Bakit rebars?
Kasi sa glass block construction, lalo na sa panel installation, may reinforcement sa joints para tumulong sa stability ng panel. Sa actual practice, puwedeng metal reinforcement wires or bars ang gamitin sa mortar joints. Sa choices na binigay, ang pinaka-tamang structural reinforcement answer ay rebars.
Ito ang clue sa tanong:
Ang tanong ay what reinforcement is used.
Ibig sabihin, huwag kang pipili ng bonding material lang o finishing material lang. Dapat ang hanapin mo ay metal reinforcement.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Sealants pang-seal ng joints iyon, hindi pampalakas ng panel.
White cement binder/finish material, hindi reinforcement.
Cement grout pang-fill at bonding, pero hindi rin reinforcement.
Elimination method
Ganito mag-isip sa ALE:
Kapag ang tanong ay may salitang reinforcement, alisin mo agad ang lahat ng hindi bakal o metal support.
So tanggal:
•	sealants
•	white cement
•	cement grout
Natitira: rebars.
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan madadala ka sa material na “ginagamit sa glass block” pero hindi lahat ng ginagamit ay reinforcement.
Ang tanong ay hindi “anong material ang kasama,” kundi anong reinforcement.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Reinforcement means pampalakas, hindi pang-seal o pang-bond lang.
Sa choices na ito, rebars ang tama.

question: Where is glass blocks not used?
keyMemory: Glass blocks are usually for non-load-bearing walls/partitions, not structural columns.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. columns
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay columns.
Bakit columns?
Kasi ang glass blocks are generally non-load-bearing units. Maganda sila for light-admitting walls, partitions, decorative panels, at some special floor or pavement applications in certain systems. Pero hindi sila ginagamit bilang structural column. Ang column kasi load-bearing vertical element iyan. Hindi iyan ang trabaho ng glass blocks.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Hanapin mo agad kung alin sa choices ang structural load-bearing element.
Walls and partitions — puwede.
Floors — may special glass floor/block applications.
Columns — dito ka dapat magduda agad.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Walls yes, common use iyan ng glass blocks.
Partition yes, very common din.
Floors puwedeng gamitin in special forms tulad ng glass paving/light-admitting floor systems, kaya hindi ito ang “not used” answer sa reviewer logic.
Elimination method
Kapag may material na kilala mong non-load-bearing, alisin mo ang choice na nangangailangan ng load-bearing structural behavior.
Dito, iyon si column.
Common trap sa board exam:
Puwedeng mauto ka sa floors, kasi iniisip mo agad wall material lang ang glass blocks. Pero ang mas malinaw na hindi talaga niya role ay column.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Glass blocks are usually for non-load-bearing walls/partitions, not structural columns.

answerConceptVisual.title: Glass block limitation
answerConceptVisual.detail: Glass blocks are wall/light-transmitting units, not structural column material.

question: A method of forming metal wherein holes are drilled in flat surfaces
keyMemory: Perforating = paggawa ng mga butas sa flat metal surface.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Perforating
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Perforating.
Bakit perforating?
Kasi ang perforating is the process of making multiple holes or patterned openings in sheet or flat metal surfaces. Ang wording ng tanong na “holes are drilled in flat surfaces” points more strongly to perforating kaysa sa ibang choices.
Bakit hindi piercing?
Magkalapit sila, kaya ito ang trap.
Piercing usually refers to punching or making an opening in sheet metal, often as a specific hole-forming operation.
Pero kapag ang wording ay mas general at parang series or pattern of holes in flat surfaces, mas expected reviewer answer ang perforating.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Blanking is cutting out a piece from sheet metal.
Bending changes shape, not hole-making.
Elimination method
Kapag ang key action ay butas sa flat sheet, compare mo agad:
•	hole-making ba?
•	shape-changing ba?
•	piece-cutting ba?
Tanggalin agad:
•	blanking
•	bending
Then pagpilian mo si piercing at perforating.
Kapag plural ang dating ng wording — holes sa flat surfaces — mas lamang si perforating.
Common trap sa board exam:
Piercing and perforating sound almost the same.
Ang safe clue dito ay: perforating = maraming butas / patterned holes.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Perforating = paggawa ng mga butas sa flat metal surface.

question: Used to unite two metal plates by passing it through a hole in both plates and then hammering down the point to form a second head.
keyMemory: Rivet forms a second head after installation. Bolt does not.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Rivet
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Rivet.
Ito classic definition ng rivet.
Ang rivet ay pinapadaan sa hole ng dalawang plates, then the projecting end is hammered or formed para magkaroon ng second head, kaya  
Bakit hindi bolt?
Ang bolt uses threads and nut. Hindi mo hinahammer down para gumawa ng second head.
Bakit hindi fasten?
Masyadong generic na verb or general term iyan, hindi specific hardware.
Tox hindi iyan ang tamang standard answer dito.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	through a hole
•	hammering down the point
•	form a second head
Kapag nakita mo ang phrase na second head, halos automatic na rivet iyan.
Common trap sa board exam:
Pag metal connection, maraming examinee agad bolt ang iniisip.
Pero tandaan:
bolt = may nut
rivet = deform the end to form another head
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rivet forms a second head after installation. Bolt does not.

question: In welding stainless steel, the type of rod to use is:
keyMemory: Sa ganitong reviewer item, match the welding rod to the base metal. Stainless steel = SS welding rod.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. SS welding rod
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay SS welding rod.
Bakit?
Simple material compatibility logic ito.
Kung stainless steel ang iwi-weld mo, ang expected filler/welding rod sa ganitong reviewer question ay stainless steel welding rod o SS welding rod.
Bakit mali ang iba?
GI welding rod — GI means galvanized iron, hindi ito ang tamang expected answer for stainless steel.
W welding rod — hindi standard at mukhang distractor lang.
BI welding rod — hindi rin ito ang correct material match dito.
Elimination method
Kapag welding question na ganito ka-direct, gamitin mo ang material matching logic:
•	stainless steel → stainless steel rod
•	huwag pumili ng rod na para sa ibang metal system
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan sobrang simple ng tanong kaya nag-o-overthink ang examinee.
Dito, huwag mo nang pahirapan.
SS material = SS rod ang exam logic.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong reviewer item, match the welding rod to the base metal.
Stainless steel = SS welding rod.

question: The two (2) general types of welding are:
keyMemory: Sa basic welding theory, two general types = pressure welding and fusion welding.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. pressure and fusion
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay pressure and fusion.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa basic welding classification, ang dalawang malalaking grupo ay:
Pressure welding — pinagdurugtong ang metal with pressure, kadalasan may kasamang init o wala, depende sa process.
Fusion welding — ang joint ay ginagawa sa pamamagitan ng pagkatunaw ng base metal, madalas may filler, tapos nagfu-fuse sila habang lumalamig.
Iyan ang broad, textbook-style classification. Kaya kapag ang tanong ay general types, ang hanap mo ay iyong pinaka-malawak na categories, hindi iyong specific method lang.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. arc and electric — mali agad kasi ang arc welding ay isa nang uri ng electric welding. Hindi sila magka-level na categories.
c. solder and bracing — una, “bracing” ay hindi welding type. Baka typo pa nga ang reviewer at gustong sabihin ay brazing, pero kahit ganoon, soldering at brazing are metal-joining methods, not the two general classes of welding.
d. gas and electric — these are ways of heating or sources/process families, pero hindi sila ang two broadest general classes na hinahanap ng tanong.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag may phrase na general types, huwag kang pipili ng masyadong specific.
So alisin mo agad:
•	arc and electric, kasi overlapping at hindi clean classification
•	solder and bracing, kasi hindi tamang pair
•	gas and electric, kasi source/method grouping iyan, not the broadest theory grouping
Ang natitira at pinaka-mataas ang level of classification: pressure and fusion.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa basic welding theory, two general types = pressure welding and fusion welding.

question: Who is the highly trained worker that installs metal roofs and gutters?
keyMemory: Latero = sheet-metal worker for yero, flashing, gutters, roofing accessories.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. latero
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay latero.
Bakit latero?
Sa local construction usage, ang latero ang specialist sa yero, flashing, gutters, downspouts, at iba pang sheet-metal roof accessories. Siya ang mas direct na skilled worker for metal roofing and gutter installation.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Carpintero or carpentero — woodwork at general carpentry ang association nito, hindi sheet-metal roofing specialist.
Tubero — plumber, piping and water lines iyan.
Alahero — jeweler, obviously hindi ito construction roof installer.
Elimination method
Ganito ang mabilis na diskarte:
Tingnan mo kung aling choice ang trade-specific sa yero o sheet metal.
Tanggalin mo agad:
•	carpintero, kasi kahoy/general framing
•	tubero, kasi plumbing
•	alahero, kasi jewelry
Automatic matitira si latero.
Common trap sa board exam:
Dahil may roof at framing sa isip ng examinee, minsan napipili ang carpintero. Pero ang clue dito ay metal roofs and gutters, hindi wood roof framing.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Latero = sheet-metal worker for yero, flashing, gutters, roofing accessories.

question: What is the English term for latero?
keyMemory: Latero = tinsmith sa reviewer terminology.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Tinsmith
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Tinsmith.
Bakit tinsmith?
Ang traditional English term for a worker dealing with light sheet metal, tulad ng roofing sheets, gutters, flashings, and similar fabricated metal pieces, ay tinsmith. Sa local practice, iyan ang pinakamalapit na katumbas ng latero.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Blacksmith — mas associated sa forging heavy iron by heating and hammering. Ibang trade iyon.
Steelsmith — hindi ito ang standard common term sa ganitong reviewer context.
Metalsmith — too broad. Puwede sa maraming metal crafts, pero hindi ito ang specific trade term na hinahanap.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay English equivalent ng latero, hanapin ang specific historical trade term, hindi generic na “metal worker.”
So ang pinaka-precise choice: tinsmith.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Latero = tinsmith sa reviewer terminology.

ROOF TYPES
visualReviewNote: Building Construction review-coach mode: no question image needed for this item; kept text/concept review only to avoid visual clues and remote-image failures.

question: A kind of roof that has four sloping sides
keyMemory: Hip roof = four sloping sides. Huwag malito agad sa mansard unless sinabi ang two slopes per side.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Hip Roof
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hip Roof.
Bakit hip roof?
Ang defining feature ng hip roof ay apat ang sloping sides, all sides slope downward toward the walls, and wala itong vertical gable ends like a gable roof.
Bakit hindi mansard?
Ito ang common trap.
Oo, ang mansard also has slopes on all four sides, pero ang pinaka-distinctive identity nito ay double slope on each side. Kapag simple at generic ang description na four sloping sides, the cleaner answer is hip roof.
Kung sinabi sana ng tanong na each side has two slopes, doon ka pupunta sa mansard.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Gable roof — two sloping sides lang at may gable ends.
Lean-to — single slope lang iyan.
Elimination method
Keyword watch:
•	four sloping sides = hip
•	two sloping sides = gable
•	one slope = lean-to
•	double slope on each side = mansard
Kaya mabilis ang elimination dito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Hip roof = four sloping sides.
Huwag malito agad sa mansard unless sinabi ang two slopes per side.

question: A type of roof with a modification of gable roof with each sides having two slopes?
keyMemory: Gambrel = modified gable, two slopes each side. Mansard = modified hip, two slopes each side.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Gambrel Roof
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Gambrel Roof.
Ito ang magandang pair sa previous question.
Bakit gambrel?
Ang gambrel roof ay parang modified gable roof na ang bawat side ay may dalawang slope — upper flatter slope and lower steeper slope. Karaniwan mo itong nakikita sa barn-type forms.
Ito ang pinakaimportanteng clue:
modification of gable roof
Ibig sabihin, gable-based pa rin ang form niya, hindi hip-based.
Bakit hindi mansard?
Ito ang pinakamalaking trap.
Ang mansard roof ay may two slopes on each of four sides, so mas tama siyang isipin bilang modified hip roof, hindi modified gable roof.
Samantalang ang gambrel has two slopes on each of two sides only, kaya siya ang modified gable roof.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Hip roof has four single slopes, not two slopes per side.
Hip-gable roof hybrid form iyan, not the classic answer to this definition.
Elimination method
Ito ang exam shortcut:
•	Gable + two slopes each side = Gambrel
•	Hip + two slopes each side = Mansard
Kapag na-master mo iyan, hindi ka na malilito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Gambrel = modified gable, two slopes each side.
Mansard = modified hip, two slopes each side.

question: A trough used for carrying off water
keyMemory: Gutter = horizontal trough that carries roof water. Downspout = vertical pipe that brings it down.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Gutter
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Gutter.
Bakit gutter?
Kasi ang gutter ang horizontal trough placed along the eaves or roof edge para saluhin at idirekta ang rainwater papunta sa discharge point.
Bakit hindi downspout?
Ang downspout ay vertical pipe, hindi trough.
Ang tanong ay specifically a trough. Iyan ang pinaka-importanteng clue.
Bakit hindi valley duet?
Mukhang typo ito ng reviewer, baka valley duct or valley gutter ang gusto nilang isulat. Pero kahit ganoon, ang pangkalahatang standard answer sa phrase na “a trough used for carrying off water” ay gutter.
Moat obvious na hindi roof-drainage term.
Elimination method
Hanapin ang shape/function clue:
•	trough = horizontal collector
•	pipe = downspout
So tanggal agad si downspout.
Then between gutter and that odd valley term, piliin mo ang mas general, standard, and clean answer: gutter.
Common trap sa board exam:
Kapag may typo o kakaibang term ang reviewer, huwag mag-panic. Piliin mo ang pinaka-standard at pinaka-malinis na construction term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Gutter = horizontal trough that carries roof water.
Downspout = vertical pipe that brings it down.

Sige, Page 11 tayo. Medyo maraming roof/truss terms dito, so ang gagawin natin ay i-translate natin sa utak ng board taker — ano ang hahanapin mong clue, ano ang tatanggalin mong mali, at paano mo maaabot ang tamang sagot kahit hindi ka 100% sure.

question: A principal member of the truss which extends from one end to the other primarily to resist bending is a
keyMemory: Chord = principal longitudinal member of the truss.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Chord
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Chord.
Bakit chord?
Sa truss, ang principal members na tumatakbo from one end to the other are the top chord and bottom chord. Sila ang main longitudinal members ng truss. Ang web members nasa loob lang at pang-triangulation and load transfer. Kaya kapag sinabing principal member extending from one end to the other, ang hanap mo ay chord.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Web member — internal member lang iyan, hindi full-length principal line.
Girt — wall framing member iyon, not the principal truss member here.
King post — specific vertical member in some truss types, but not the one extending from one end to the other.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay main member ng truss, isipin mo agad:
•	top chord
•	bottom chord
So kung may choice na chord, iyan ang pinakamalakas na sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Chord = principal longitudinal member of the truss.

question: Identify which letter in the target is a valley jack rafter?
keyMemory: Valley jack rafter = short rafter na nakakabit sa valley rafter.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. B
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay B.
Bakit B?
Ang valley jack rafter ay short rafter na tumatakbo mula ridge papunta sa valley rafter. Hindi ito iyong full-length common rafter, at hindi rin ito iyong valley line mismo. Sa drawing, ang label na B ang tumuturo sa short rafter members na nakakabit sa valley side near the ridge area.
Ito ang clue sa figure reading:
Hanapin mo ang:
•	valley line muna
•	tapos yung maiikling rafters na kumakabit dito
Iyan ang valley jack rafters.
Bakit mali ang iba?
F mas mukhang valley line or lower valley area mismo.
C mas mukhang common or outer rafter zone.
G nasa lower eave/plate side area, not the valley jack.
Elimination method
Sa roof-framing figure:
•	Hip rafter = diagonal from corner to ridge
•	Valley rafter = diagonal inside angle
•	Valley jack = short rafters connecting to valley rafter
Kaya ang short member near the valley ang hahanapin mo, at dito iyon si B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Valley jack rafter = short rafter na nakakabit sa valley rafter.

question: Rafters laid diagonally from corner of a plate or girts to the ridge
keyMemory: Outside corner to ridge = hip rafter. Inside intersection to ridge = valley rafter.

Correct answer: d. Hip rafter

Ito very classic roof-framing definition.
Kapag ang rafter ay diagonal from the outside corner ng plate papunta sa ridge, iyan ay hip rafter.
Bakit hindi valley rafter?
Ang valley rafter ay nasa inside angle or internal intersection ng roof slopes.
Samantalang ang hip rafter ay nasa outside angle/corner.

Jack rafter short rafter lang iyon.
Valley plate hindi iyan ang tamang term dito.

•	corner = hip
•	inside intersection = valley
Kapag may word na corner sa tanong, malakas agad si hip rafter.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Outside corner to ridge = hip rafter.
Inside intersection to ridge = valley rafter.

question: Longitudinal beams which rest on the top chord and preferably at the joints of the truss
keyMemory: Purlins sit on trusses and support roofing.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Purlins
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Purlins.
Bakit purlins?
Ang purlins ang longitudinal members na sumusuporta sa roofing material at karaniwang nakapatong sa top chords ng trusses. Tama rin ang clue na preferably at the joints of the truss dahil doon mas maayos ang load transfer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Jack rafters roof-framing members iyan, not longitudinal truss-top members.
Rafters sloping framing members, hindi sila iyong longitudinal members resting across trusses.
Girders mas malalaki at ibang structural context.
Elimination method
Kapag usapan ay member carrying roof sheets across trusses, isipin mo agad purlins.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Purlins sit on trusses and support roofing.

question: Roofing metal truss system shall be exposed and will be used as an interior element, which fastening system should you use?
keyMemory: Sa exposed steel na architectural ang dating, cleaner connection = full weld ang usual reviewer answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Full weld
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Full weld.
Bakit full weld?
Kapag ang metal truss ay exposed as an interior element, malaking issue ang appearance. Ang full weld gives a cleaner, more continuous, and more finished-looking connection kumpara sa visible bolts or rivets. Sa reviewer logic, ito ang mas elegant at mas “architectural” na fastening/joining choice for exposed steel.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Bolts visible heads and plates — puwede, pero less visually clean.
Rivets visible din, at mas old-style detailing.
Gussets hindi fastening system by itself; connection plate iyon, not the actual fastening method.
Elimination method
Kapag may clue na exposed interior element, isipin mo agad ang clean finish at aesthetic integration.
Sa choices, ang pinakabagay doon ay full weld.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa exposed steel na architectural ang dating, cleaner connection = full weld ang usual reviewer answer.

answerConceptVisual.title: Exposed metal truss fastening
answerConceptVisual.detail: Because the truss system is exposed and intended as an interior element, full weld gives a cleaner and more rigid connection.

question: Standard spacing between metal C-purlins on steel trusses?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer memory peg: metal C-purlins for steel trusses = 0.70 m o.c.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 0.70 m o.c.
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 0.70 meter o.c.
Ito reviewer-memory item ito. Sa ordinary steel truss with metal roofing, the usual standard spacing answer in these reviewers is 0.70 m o.c.
Bakit hindi 0.30?
Masyadong dikit iyan for ordinary metal-sheet roofing and more associated with tile-support spacing.
0.50 and 0.60 can appear in practice depending on roofing system, pero sa reviewer style na ito, 0.70 ang standard answer.
Elimination method
Ipares mo agad ito sa next question:
•	ordinary steel truss metal roofing = 0.70 m o.c.
•	roofing tile = mas dikit
So dito, 0.70 ang natitira.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer memory peg: metal C-purlins for steel trusses = 0.70 m o.c.

Fillet is a small strip/filling piece in many contexts, but the classic board-joint cover term here is batten.

question: Identify the roof seam:
keyMemory: Sa seam identification, bantayan mo kung: • simpleng lap lang • isang lock • dobleng lock • o snap profile Dito, single lock ang natitira.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Single lock
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Single lock.
Batay sa sketch, ang seam shown is a standing seam with a single folded interlock, not a plain overlap and not the more tightly folded double-lock form. Hindi rin mukhang snap-lock profile.
Bakit hindi overlap?
Because the drawing clearly shows an upright interlocked seam, not just one sheet overlapping another flat.
Bakit hindi double lock?
Sa double lock, mas pronounced ang double-folded compressed seam.
Bakit hindi snap lock?
Snap lock usually has a different profile with male-female snap action rather than this simple folded lock look.
Elimination method
Sa roof seam images:
•	Overlap = simple flat lap
•	Single lock = one folded standing seam
•	Double lock = tighter double-fold seam
•	Snap lock = snap-profile system
Since iisa lang ang evident fold/interlock sa figure, single lock ang best answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa seam identification, bantayan mo kung:
•	simpleng lap lang
•	isang lock
•	dobleng lock
•	o snap profile
Dito, single lock ang natitira.

Sige, Page 12 tayo. Medyo practical roofing at waterproofing items ito, tapos may seam identification pa. Ang importante rito, huwag kang ma-intimidate sa pictures. Hanapin mo lang kung ano ang pinaka-distinctive clue.

question: Identify the roof seam:
keyMemory: Kapag raised rectangular seam with clip, think snap-on batten with clip.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Snap-on batten with clip
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Snap-on batten with clip.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Tingnan mo ang drawing: ang profile niya ay parang raised rectangular batten cover na nakasnap sa clips. Hindi lang siya simpleng snap lock seam, at hindi rin siya iyong ordinaryong batten na may solid filler sa loob.
Ang clue dito ay:
•	may clip
•	may cover
•	raised batten-type profile
Kaya ang pinakamalapit at pinakatamang sagot ay snap-on batten with clip.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Snap lock usually mas simple at mas direct lock profile lang, hindi ganitong batten-like raised cover.
Batten alone usually tumutukoy sa plain batten system, not necessarily snap-on with clip.
Snap-on cap with clip malapit ito, pero mas bagay iyon sa cap profile kaysa sa batten-type raised seam na nasa drawing.
Elimination method
Kapag image item:
1.	Tanungin mo: simple seam lang ba ito o batten-type raised seam?
2.	Hanapin kung may clip.
3.	Kapag may clip at batten-looking cap, piliin ang may batten with clip.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag raised rectangular seam with clip, think snap-on batten with clip.

question: The correct way of laying concrete roof tiles is
keyMemory: Roof tiles are laid from the gutter/eaves up to the ridge.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. from gutter to ridge
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay from gutter to ridge.
Bakit?
Sa roof tile installation, inuumpisahan mo sa ibaba at umaakyat ka pataas para tama ang lapping at maayos ang water-shedding direction.
So from gutter/eaves upward to ridge ang tamang sequence.
Bakit mali ang iba?
From ridge to gutter baliktad ang practical laying logic.
From raker to raker side-to-side lang iyan, hindi nito sinasagot ang main vertical laying sequence.
Any manner obvious na mali.
Elimination method
Think like water:
Dapat ang upper tile ay naka-overlap sa lower sequence properly habang umaakyat ka.
So installation starts from the lowest course upward.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Roof tiles are laid from the gutter/eaves up to the ridge.

question: What is the use of a gap in skylight construction?
keyMemory: Construction gaps often exist to allow temperature expansion and contraction.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. for temperature expansion
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay for temperature expansion.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa skylight construction, may iba’t ibang materials na exposed sa init at lamig. Kapag walang allowance, puwedeng mag-crack ang glass or ma-stress ang frame. Kaya ang gap is mainly to allow thermal movement / temperature expansion.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Air venting at heat escape puwedeng effects sa ibang design contexts, pero ang specific reason for construction gap is movement allowance.
Filtering obviously hindi iyon.
Elimination method
Kapag may tanong sa construction gap, isipin mo agad:
“Bakit nag-iiwan ng allowance sa detail?”
Usually dahil sa movement — especially thermal movement.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Construction gaps often exist to allow temperature expansion and contraction.

question: The capacity of a wall to hold moisture is important in the design of dwelling units. Select the best material which will reduce moisture in a wall when used

choices.text: Synthetic adobe finish on R.C. wall

Bakit brick siding?

Bakit mali ang iba?
CHB 5-cut joint finish hindi iyan moisture-reducing cladding solution.
Y-cut wood boards puwede in some assemblies, pero hindi iyan ang pinakamatibay na board-review answer for reducing wall moisture.
Synthetic adobe finish on RC wall finish lang iyan, hindi best moisture-reducing choice sa listahan.

Kapag moisture reduction in wall ang concern, mas malakas ang protective siding/cladding answer kaysa ordinary finish lang.

question: It is necessary to provide ____ to turn back water whenever joints occur in which dissimilar materials come together.
keyMemory: General water-deflecting detail at joints = flashing material. Counter-flashing is only a more specific subtype.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Flashing material
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Flashing material.
Bakit flashing material?
Ito ang general material/detail used to turn back water at joints, especially where dissimilar materials meet — halimbawa roof to wall, masonry to metal, concrete to frame transitions, and similar vulnerable points.
Bakit hindi counter-flashing?
Ito ang common trap.
Counter-flashing is a more specific kind of flashing used in certain details, usually with base flashing. Pero ang tanong ay general statement tungkol sa joints of dissimilar materials. So the broader and more correct answer is flashing material.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Binding material not for turning back water.
Grouting fills gaps, but not the general water-turnback detail asked.
Elimination method
Kapag may tanong na:
•	water control
•	joints
•	dissimilar materials
First answer to think of is flashing.
Only go to counter-flashing if the detail is specifically that kind of layered flashing assembly.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
General water-deflecting detail at joints = flashing material.
Counter-flashing is only a more specific subtype.

question: When the soil beneath the building is not exceptionally well drained and it is necessary to exclude dampness, the best material to be used which is installed below the concrete slab is:
keyMemory: Under-slab moisture barrier = plastic sheet ang mabilis na reviewer answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Plastic sheet
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Plastic sheet.
Bakit plastic sheet?
Ang clue dito ay “installed below the concrete slab” at ang purpose ay exclude dampness. Ibig sabihin, ang hinahanap ay moisture barrier or vapor barrier sa ilalim ng slab. Sa practical construction at reviewer logic, iyan ang role ng plastic sheet, usually polyethylene sheet.
Bakit hindi gravel course?
Maganda ang gravel for drainage and breaking capillary rise to some extent, pero hindi siya ang pinaka-direct moisture barrier na “pangharang” ng dampness.
Bakit hindi aluminum foil?
Hindi iyan ang standard under-slab damp-proofing material.
Bakit hindi pitch or bituminous felt?
Puwedeng damp-proofing material iyan sa ibang assemblies, pero sa ilalim ng slab, ang mas direct at expected reviewer answer ay plastic sheet.
Elimination method
Kapag may phrase na:
•	below slab
•	exclude dampness
•	best material
Hanapin mo ang sheet barrier.
Hindi drain layer, hindi decorative material, hindi unusual foil.
So ang natitira ay plastic sheet.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Under-slab moisture barrier = plastic sheet ang mabilis na reviewer answer.

question: A waterproofing treatment should be specified in lieu of a dampproofing treatment for subsurface masonry when which of the following conditions is present?
keyMemory: Moisture lang = dampproofing. Water pressure = waterproofing.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Hydrostatic pressure
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Hydrostatic pressure.
Ito ang very important distinction:
Dampproofing is for resisting ordinary moisture.
Waterproofing is needed when may water pressure na tumutulak laban sa wall or subsurface element.
Kapag may hydrostatic pressure, hindi na sapat ang ordinary dampproofing lang. Kailangan mo ng mas heavy-duty waterproofing system.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Low frost line hindi ito ang main issue sa Philippine ALE context, at hindi iyan ang trigger condition for choosing waterproofing over dampproofing.
Sandy soils puwedeng well-draining pa nga minsan.
Expansive soils concern iyan structurally, but not the direct reason for upgrading from damp-proofing to waterproofing.
Elimination method
Tanungin mo sarili mo:
“Kailan ba hindi sapat ang damp-proofing?”
Kapag may actual water pressure na.
Water pressure = hydrostatic pressure.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Moisture lang = dampproofing.
Water pressure = waterproofing.

answerConceptVisual.title: Waterproofing vs dampproofing
answerConceptVisual.main: Use waterproofing when hydrostatic pressure is present
answerConceptVisual.detail: Hydrostatic pressure means water under pressure; dampproofing alone is not enough.

question: The passage of water under pressure through concrete cracks is defined as:
keyMemory: Water through cracks = leakage. Water through fine pores = seepage.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Leakage
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Leakage.
Bakit leakage?
Ang tanong ay very specific: water under pressure through concrete cracks.
Kapag through cracks or openings ang pasok ng tubig, mas tamang term diyan ay leakage.
Seepage is usually more associated with slow movement through pores or fine paths in material.
Pero kapag may actual crack path at under pressure ang tubig, leakage ang expected reviewer answer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Saturation means the material is fully soaked or filled with water, not the act of passage.
Capillary action is upward or fine-void movement due to surface tension, not water under pressure through cracks.
Elimination method
Tingnan mo ang path ng water:
•	through pores or tiny voids = seepage
•	through crack/opening = leakage
Board trap ito dahil magkalapit ang seepage at leakage.
Ang clue para hindi ka malito ay ang word na cracks.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Water through cracks = leakage.
Water through fine pores = seepage.

THERMAL INSULATION

answerConceptVisual.title: Water under pressure through cracks
answerConceptVisual.detail: Passage of pressurized water through concrete cracks is leakage.

question: Heat gain through the walls in buildings exposed to afternoon sun intensifies discomfort of inhabitants because of conductivity. However, this can be minimized by the use of

choices.text: combination of wood boards and plywood with cavity between

answer: a. combination of wood boards and plywood with cavity between
keyMemory: Air cavity helps reduce heat transfer. Sa thermal insulation questions, bantayan mo agad ang word na cavity.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. combination of wood boards and plywood with cavity between
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay combination of wood boards and plywood with cavity between.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue dito ay conductivity. Gusto mong bawasan ang paglipat ng init through the wall. Ang pinaka-effective sa choices ay iyong may cavity or trapped air space. Bakit? Kasi ang air space helps reduce direct heat transfer.
Bakit hindi thick reinforced concrete wall?
RC absorbs and stores heat. Hindi iyan ang best answer para bawasan ang conductive heat gain sa choices na ito.
Bakit hindi steel sheets?
Lalong mabilis mag-conduct ng init ang steel.
Bakit hindi 1/4" cement boards?
Manipis pa, at wala ring cavity advantage tulad ng choice a.
Elimination method
Kapag conductivity ang issue, hanapin mo ang choice na may:
•	less conductive materials
•	air cavity / trapped air space
Iyan ang reason bakit natitira si a.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Air cavity helps reduce heat transfer.
Sa thermal insulation questions, bantayan mo agad ang word na cavity.

answerConceptVisual.title: Reduce heat conductivity
answerConceptVisual.main: Wood boards + plywood with cavity between
answerConceptVisual.detail: The cavity/trapped air space reduces direct heat transfer through the exterior wall.

question: Humidification and condensation in exterior walls can be minimized by

choices.text: Providing stud space between exterior finish and interior finish
Providing building paper sheathing and space filled

answer: c. Providing building paper sheathing and space filled
keyMemory: Sa moisture/condensation control, single measure often isn’t enough. Hanapin mo ang more complete wall assembly answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Providing building paper sheathing and space filled
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Providing building paper sheathing and space filled.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Ang condensation control sa wall assembly is not solved by cavity alone. Kailangan mo rin ng sheathing or barrier layer plus a more controlled wall build-up. Sa choices, ang pinaka-complete answer ay iyong may building paper sheathing and space filled.
Ito ang logic:
Kung cavity lang without control layer, puwedeng magkaroon pa rin ng moisture problems.
Kung paper sheathing lang without proper space treatment, kulang din.
So ang mas complete moisture-control answer sa reviewer logic ay c.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a cavity alone is incomplete.
b sheathing alone is incomplete.
d mali dahil may mas magandang answer sa taas.
Elimination method
Kapag may dalawang choices na parehong may parte ng solution, at may isang choice na pinagsama ang mas complete assembly, madalas iyon ang tamang sagot.
Dito, iyon si c.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa moisture/condensation control, single measure often isn’t enough. Hanapin mo ang more complete wall assembly answer.

question: Sound or noise between bedrooms may be reduced by using less expensive materials with the application of construction techniques

choices.text: double wall plywood on horizontal and vertical studs with absorbents

answer: a. double wall plywood on horizontal and vertical studs with absorbents
keyMemory: Sa sound insulation, layering + cavity + absorbent is stronger than just one thin material.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. double wall plywood on horizontal and vertical studs with absorbents
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay double wall plywood on horizontal and vertical studs with absorbents.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang tanong mismo nagsabi: less expensive materials plus construction techniques.
So huwag kang agad pupunta sa massive concrete wall. Ang hinahanap ay lighter, cheaper assembly pero improved acoustically through layering and absorbent material.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Solid 6" RC wall can reduce sound, yes, but hindi iyan ang “less expensive materials” answer.
Sawali on 2x2 studs too light and not the best sound-reducing choice.
Gypsum board alone is too incomplete as an acoustic system answer.
Elimination method
Keyword trap:
•	less expensive
•	with application of construction techniques
That means the answer should involve an assembly, not just one material.
So among the choices, a ang pinaka-buo at pinaka-acoustic ang logic.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa sound insulation, layering + cavity + absorbent is stronger than just one thin material.

question: When installing plywood on wood studs, the carpenter uses what system of measurement?

Sa practical plywood sizing, especially older and very common construction practice, ginagamit ang feet and inches dimensions like 4' x 8'. Kaya ang expected reviewer answer ay English system.

Metric system ginagamit din sa documents, yes, pero sa carpenter’s conventional plywood sizing and field use, 4x8 thinking ang common reviewer logic.
ISO hindi ito ang measurement system being asked.

4 feet by 8 feet
Since feet/inches iyan, English system ang sagot.

Plywood on site is commonly thought of as 4' x 8' sa traditional construction language.

question: Standard dimension for plywood is 1.2m x 2.4m or?

So:
1.2m x 2.4m = 48" x 96"

Tandaan mo ang basic memory peg:
1.2 x 2.4 = 4 x 8
Then convert to inches.

Standard plywood = 4' x 8' = 48" x 96"

question: A type of mortise and tenon wood construction joint where the tongue and groove meets halfway making the connection clean of joint traces:
keyMemory: If the joint should not show through the face, think blind mortise and tenon.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. blind and stub
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay blind and stub.
Bakit blind and stub?
Ang clue dito ay “clean of joint traces.”
Ibig sabihin, hindi dapat lumusot ang tenon sa kabilang face. Kapag hindi lumalabas sa other side, mas malinis ang finished face. Iyan ang logic ng blind and stub mortise and tenon.
Bakit hindi through?
Sa through mortise and tenon, lumalabas ang tenon sa kabilang side, so may visible trace.
Open or bridle joint is more exposed.
Keyed uses a key, not the exact “hidden trace” description.
Elimination method
Kapag ang clue ay clean face / walang bakas sa labas, hanapin mo ang hidden joint.
Iyan ang reason bakit natitira si blind and stub.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
If the joint should not show through the face, think blind mortise and tenon.

question: Visible choices from the continuation:
answer: d. wedged through mortise and tenon
keyMemory: Kapag may separate wedge sa mortise-and-tenon drawing, malakas ang sagot na wedged through mortise and tenon.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. wedged through mortise and tenon
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay wedged through mortise and tenon.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa drawing, may tatlong mahalagang clue:
una, may mortise opening sa main member
ikalawa, may tenon na pumapasok
ikatlo, may separate wedge piece na nakikita sa exploded view
Iyang third clue ang pinaka-importante. Kapag may wedge na ini-drive para lumapad at mas kumapit ang tenon, hindi na iyan ordinaryong mortise and tenon lang. Iyan ay wedged through mortise and tenon.
Bakit hindi blind mortise and tenon?
Sa blind mortise and tenon, hindi lumulusot ang tenon sa kabilang side, at ang emphasis doon ay hidden o clean face. Dito, ang presence ng wedge ang mas dominant clue.
Bakit hindi haunched mortise and tenon?
Ang haunched version usually may reduced portion or haunch para masuportahan ang edge or groove condition, pero hindi iyon ang pinaka-obvious na ipinapakita rito. Ang drawing mas malinaw na nagpapakita ng wedge action.
Elimination method
Kapag image-based ang mortise-and-tenon item, hanapin mo ito:
•	may tenon lang ba?
•	lumulusot ba?
•	may wedge ba?
•	hidden ba or exposed?
Kapag may wedge, iyon agad ang strongest clue.
So kahit hindi mo buo ang choices, puwede mong ma-eliminate:
blind = no visible wedge emphasis
haunched = ibang geometry ang clue
wedged through = tugma sa drawing
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may separate wedge sa mortise-and-tenon drawing, malakas ang sagot na wedged through mortise and tenon.

question: A wood splice for resisting tension
keyMemory: Scarf splice ang classic wood splice answer kapag ang clue ay tension resistance.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Scarfed
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Scarfed.
Bakit scarfed?
Sa wood splices, ang scarf splice ang kilalang ginagamit kapag kailangan mong magdugtong ng dalawang kahoy end-to-end nang may mas maayos na load transfer, especially kung may tension component. Mas mahaba ang contact surface niya kaysa simpleng butt o square splice, kaya mas may chance siyang lumaban sa paghila.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Square splice — masyadong simple, mas mahina laban sa tension.
Halved splice — puwede, pero mas hindi iyon ang classic answer for resisting tension.
Scabbed splice — may side pieces or scabs, yes, pero sa reviewer terminology, ang mas expected answer sa “resisting tension” ay scarfed.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay resisting tension, hanapin mo ang splice na:
•	mas mahaba ang contact surface
•	hindi simpleng butt lang
•	may mas refined geometry for load transfer
Diyan nangingibabaw si scarfed.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Scarf splice ang classic wood splice answer kapag ang clue ay tension resistance.

question: Which among the following has the most rigid frame?
keyMemory: Sa wood frames, mortise and tenon ang isa sa pinaka-matatag at pinaka-board-safe na sagot.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. mortise and tenon
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay mortise and tenon.
Bakit mortise and tenon?
Sa frame joinery, ito ang isa sa pinaka-classic at pinaka-matatag na joint. May actual male member na pumapasok sa female recess, kaya mas maganda ang interlock, alignment, at resistance to racking compared sa miter or simple lap.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Mitered maganda tingnan, pero hindi iyan ang pinaka-rigid by itself.
Half lap okay, pero generally less rigid kaysa mortise and tenon in frame work.
Rabbetted more for edge/recess connection, hindi iyan ang strongest frame joint sa choices.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay most rigid frame, huwag piliin ang joint na aesthetic lang or simple lap lang.
Hanapin mo ang true interlocking frame joint.
Iyon si mortise and tenon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa wood frames, mortise and tenon ang isa sa pinaka-matatag at pinaka-board-safe na sagot.

question: A joint made by inserting the end or edge of one member into a corresponding rectangular groove cut in another member?
keyMemory: Dado = groove. Rabbet = edge recess.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Dado
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Dado.
Bakit dado?
Ang dado ay rectangular groove cut into one member para tanggapin ang end or edge ng isa pang member. Ito ang exact definition ng tanong.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Miter angled meeting iyon, usually 45 degrees.
Dovetail flared interlocking shape iyon.
Rabbet open-sided recess iyon sa edge, hindi enclosed groove like dado.
Elimination method
Kapag sinabi ng tanong:
•	rectangular groove
•	pinapasok ang edge or end
Isipin mo:
•	open groove across face = dado
•	edge recess = rabbet
Ito ang common trap pair: dado vs rabbet.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Dado = groove.
Rabbet = edge recess.

question: A wood connectors are efficient when
keyMemory: Sa wood-connector questions, grain direction matters a lot. Sa reviewer logic dito, perpendicular to the grain ang sagot.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. perpendicular to the grain
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay perpendicular to the grain.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa wood construction, maraming connectors ang mas effective kapag ang force transfer at bearing action ay across the grain rather than along it. Ang wood kasi mas madaling mag-split or humina kapag mali ang relation ng connector sa grain direction. Kaya ang usual reviewer answer dito ay perpendicular to the grain.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Parallel to the grain madalas mas delikado sa splitting at hindi iyon ang usual “best” answer sa ganitong item.
Embossed along the grain hindi ito ang standard construction principle.
Tied around the wood section hindi rin ito ang technical answer na hinahanap.
Elimination method
Kapag wood behavior ang tanong, bantayan mo ang phrase na grain direction.
Ang ALE madalas nagtatanong kung saan mas stable or efficient ang connector behavior.
Sa choices, ang pinaka-standard engineering answer ay perpendicular to the grain.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa wood-connector questions, grain direction matters a lot.
Sa reviewer logic dito, perpendicular to the grain ang sagot.

answerConceptVisual.title: Wood connector efficiency
answerConceptVisual.detail: Connector/bearing action is more efficient when properly oriented relative to grain direction.

question: Used to connect truss members?
keyMemory: Truss joint connection = gusset plate ang classic answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Gusset Plate
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Gusset Plate.
Bakit gusset plate?
Ito ang classic na ginagamit sa trusses para pagdugtungin ang members sa joints. Sa truss connection points, ang gusset plate ang karaniwang flat plate na nagho-hold sa meeting members together.
Bakit hindi splice plate?
Splice plate ginagamit kapag dinudugtong ang dalawang members in line or lengthwise.
Pero ang tanong ay general truss members connection, lalo na sa joints, kaya gusset plate ang mas tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Plate connector sounds close, pero mas generic iyan.
Flashing plate obvious na pang-waterproofing/roof detail, hindi pang-truss connection.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay joints ng truss members, isipin mo agad ang triangular or flat connection plates sa node points.
That is gusset plate.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Truss joint connection = gusset plate ang classic answer.

question: The toilet bathroom floor finish is designed to be at least one inch below the bedroom floor finish. What should be the vertical distance between bedroom floor finish and top line of the 2" x 6" yacal floor joists which carry the toilet bathroom floor system assuming that 1/2" thick mosaic vitrified tiles will be used in the toilet bathroom on 4" R.C slab with membrane waterproofing, using standard acceptable measurement of materials for residential houses
keyMemory: Sa floor-drop problems, huwag raw thickness lang ang isipin. Isama ang practical build-up ng finish assembly.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. 6 ¾ inches
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 6 ¾ inches.
Dahan-dahan natin ito, kasi ito iyong type ng problem na mukhang mahaba pero kaya i-breakdown.
Ang hinahanap ay:
distance from bedroom finished floor level down to the top line of the wood joist
Given:
•	Toilet floor finish should be 1 inch lower than bedroom finish.
•	Toilet finish material = 1/2 inch mosaic vitrified tile
•	Under that is 4 inch R.C. slab
•	May membrane waterproofing, but usually very thin lang ito and not the main depth driver sa reviewer estimate.
•	The top line of joist is below the slab system.
So board-review logic:
•	4 inches R.C. slab
•	1/2 inch tile finish
•	plus standard setting bed / allowance
•	plus 1 inch drop from bedroom finish to toilet finish
Kaya ang expected total reviewer answer lands at 6 ¾ inches.
Ito ang importante:
Ang ganitong item sa reviewer usually follows accepted standard buildup allowances, not purely raw thickness lang. Kaya kung simpleng 4 + 1/2 + 1 = 5 1/2 lang ang kukunin mo, kulang iyon dahil hindi pa kasama ang practical buildup/bed allowance.
Elimination method
Ganito mag-isip:
•	3 1/4 or 3 1/2 inches: sobrang liit, automatic tanggal
•	So choice is between 6 1/4 and 6 3/4
•	Kapag may tile finish + slab + drop + standard buildup allowance, mas malamang na 6 3/4 ang reviewer answer
Common trap sa board exam:
Yung examinee nagco-compute ng pure thickness lang at nakakalimot sa setting bed / practical floor build-up allowance.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa floor-drop problems, huwag raw thickness lang ang isipin. Isama ang practical build-up ng finish assembly.

answerConceptVisual.title: Toilet floor joist level problem
answerConceptVisual.detail: Breakdown: 1 in lower toilet finish + 1/2 in tile + 4 in RC slab + allowances/waterproofing layer per reviewer logic.

question: A system of framing a building in which the studs are continuous to roof supporting second floor joists is known as
keyMemory: Continuous studs from lower level to roof = balloon framing.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Balloon framing
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Balloon framing.
Bakit balloon framing?
Ito ang classic definition niya: studs run continuously from lower level up to the roof, and the second-floor joists are supported off those continuous studs.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Post and beam ibang structural system iyon.
Western framing is more associated with platform framing in many reviewer references.
Post and lintel totally different structural concept.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	studs are continuous
•	to roof
•	supporting second floor joists
That is textbook balloon framing.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Continuous studs from lower level to roof = balloon framing.

question: Also called, platform framing, a system of framing a building on which floor joists of each storey rest on the top plates of the storey below and the bearing walls and partitions rest on the subfloor of each storey is known as
keyMemory: Platform framing = Western framing sa reviewer vocabulary.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Western framing
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Western framing.
Bakit western framing?
Ang description ay exactly platform framing:
•	floor joists rest on top plates of storey below
•	each storey is framed as its own platform
•	walls and partitions rest on the subfloor of that storey
Sa reviewer terminology, platform framing is also called western framing.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Balloon framing opposite logic iyon — continuous studs, not storey-by-storey platform construction.
Plank framing not the expected equivalent here.
Floor framing too generic.
Elimination method
Pair this in your head:
•	Balloon framing = continuous studs
•	Platform framing = western framing
Kapag storey-by-storey ang description, huwag piliin ang balloon framing.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Platform framing = Western framing sa reviewer vocabulary.

question: Which of the ff. describes frame construction as a structural system?

choices.text: results in a cage-like arrangement of parts through repetition

answer: d. all of the other choices
keyMemory: Sa frame construction, may vertical posts, horizontal spanning members, at repetitive structural grid. Kaya dito, all of the other choices ang tama.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. all of the other choices
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay all of the other choices.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi lahat ng nasa a, b, at c ay tunay na descriptions ng frame construction.
Una, use of upright post — tama, dahil frame systems rely on vertical members like posts or columns.
Pangalawa, spanned by horizontal elements — tama rin, kasi may beams, girders, joists, rafters na nagbi-bridge ng spaces.
Pangatlo, results in a cage-like arrangement of parts through repetition — tama rin ito, dahil frame construction often looks like repeated structural bays or repeated vertical-horizontal pattern.
So kapag lahat ng choices valid, all of the other choices ang tama.
Bakit hindi ang iba?
Hindi sila “mali,” pero kulang sila individually. Ang tanong ay which describes frame construction as a structural system — at mas complete ang sagot na pinagsama silang lahat.
Elimination method
Ganito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Basahin mo isa-isa ang a, b, c.
Tanungin mo: “Totoo ba ito sa frame construction?”
Kapag oo lahat, huwag matakot sa all of the above / all of the other choices.
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan umiiwas ang examinee sa all-of-the-above dahil feeling nila trick. Pero kung grounded ang bawat choice, iyon talaga ang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa frame construction, may vertical posts, horizontal spanning members, at repetitive structural grid. Kaya dito, all of the other choices ang tama.

answerConceptVisual.title: Frame construction
answerConceptVisual.detail: Uses upright posts, horizontal spanning members, and repeated cage-like arrangement.

question: A horizontal timber at the bottom of the frame of a wood structure, which rests on the foundation
keyMemory: Sill or sill plate ang wood member na direktang nakapatong sa foundation.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Sill
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Sill.
Bakit sill?
Ang sill o sill plate ang horizontal wood member na nakapatong sa foundation at siyang base ng wood framing above. Sa residential wood framing, very standard ito: the sill plate anchors the wall framing to the foundation.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Joist supports floor or ceiling loads, hindi siya iyong timber resting directly on the foundation.
Base masyadong generic. Hindi ito ang technical term na gusto ng tanong.
Girt usually horizontal member in wall framing systems, especially in some timber/post frames, pero hindi ito ang classic bottom member resting directly on foundation.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	horizontal timber
•	bottom of the frame
•	rests on the foundation
Kapag ganyan ang wording, isipin mo agad sill plate.
So sa choices, sill ang natitira.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sill or sill plate ang wood member na direktang nakapatong sa foundation.

question: That part of the building foundation which forms the permanent retaining wall of the structure below grade is
keyMemory: Below-grade wall that retains earth = foundation wall.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Foundation wall
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Foundation wall.
Bakit foundation wall?
Very direct ang wording: permanent retaining wall of the structure below grade. Iyan mismo ang description ng foundation wall, lalo na iyong portion na humahawak sa earth around basement or below-grade portions.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Foundation course not the correct structural wall term here.
Grade beam is a beam, not the retaining wall itself.
Shear wall resists lateral loads, but the question is specifically about the below-grade retaining wall function.
Elimination method
Kapag may phrase na below grade retaining wall, huwag kang pumunta agad sa shear wall dahil structural-lateral term iyon.
Hanapin mo ang mas direct substructure term: foundation wall.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Below-grade wall that retains earth = foundation wall.
