question: For a 30 storey building, what is the minimum dimension of a column on the 25th floor if the span of the beam is 6-8 meters?
keyMemory: Sa ganitong ALE item, hanapin ang most reasonable middle answer — hindi tiny, hindi oversized.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 0.55 x 0.65
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 0.55 x 0.65.
Ito ay reviewer-type proportioning question, hindi full structural design. Ang hinahanap dito ay reasonable preliminary column size sa upper floors of a tall building, not the huge lower-floor columns and not the tiny residential-scale columns.
Bakit 0.55 x 0.65?
Para sa 25th floor ng 30-storey building, mataas pa rin ang load pero significantly less na kaysa lower levels. So kailangan mo pa rin ng substantial column, pero hindi na kasinglaki ng podium or base columns.
Sa choices:
•	1.2 x 1.2 at 1.6 x 1.0 sobrang laki na para sa 25th floor preliminary reviewer logic
•	0.30 x 0.28 sobrang liit para sa ganitong building scale
•	kaya ang most reasonable middle answer is 0.55 x 0.65
Elimination method
Ganito mag-isip:
•	Very tall building ito, so alisin ang sobrang liit
•	Upper floor ito, so alisin din ang sobrang laki na pang-lower floors
•	Hanapin ang middle realistic size
That leaves 0.55 x 0.65.
Common trap sa board exam:
Overthinking na parang full structural design na agad. Pero reviewer question ito — more on reasonable preliminary size range.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong ALE item, hanapin ang most reasonable middle answer — hindi tiny, hindi oversized.

answerConceptVisual.title: Preliminary column sizing
answerConceptVisual.detail: Reviewer-type proportioning answer for 25th floor of a 30-storey building with 6–8 m beam span.

question: Economical spacing between two reinforced concrete columns
keyMemory: Sa ordinary RC framing, moderate spacing is most economical — dito, 12'-14' o.c.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. 12'-14' on center
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 12'-14' on center.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa general building construction reviewer logic, ang economical spacing ng RC columns is usually in a moderate range — hindi sobrang sikip, hindi sobrang layo.
12' to 14' o.c. is the practical middle ground: good structural economy without requiring overly heavy beams/slabs.
Bakit mali ang iba?
12'-18' masyadong wide ang range at puwedeng less precise.
14'-16' puwedeng workable pero usually less economical na for ordinary RC framing compared to 12'-14'.
16'-20' mas likely na long-span territory na, requiring heavier framing.
Elimination method
Keyword: economical spacing
Kapag economical, think moderate, not extreme.
•	too close = more columns, more footing, more obstruction
•	too far = heavier beams/slabs
So ang sweet spot sa reviewer logic is 12'-14'.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ordinary RC framing, moderate spacing is most economical — dito, 12'-14' o.c.

answerConceptVisual.title: Economical RC column spacing
answerConceptVisual.detail: Reviewer memory item: economical spacing between two reinforced concrete columns.

question: Columns in which a concrete core is reinforced with a steel or cast-iron core designed to support a part of load is
keyMemory: Concrete + steel core working together = composite column.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Composite
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Composite.
Bakit composite?
Ang description ay halong concrete + steel or cast-iron core supporting load together. Iyan ang very definition ng composite column.
Bakit hindi lally?
Ito ang trap.
A lally column is a specific kind of steel column often filled with concrete, but the broader technical category described by the question is composite. Since the wording is generic and definitional, go for the category, not the special trade term.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Cast-in-place method iyon, not the column type being defined.
Ready-mixed concrete supply type iyon, not a column classification.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay nagde-describe ng combined materials acting together structurally, think composite.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Concrete + steel core working together = composite column.

question: A rigid, relatively slender structural member designed primarily to support axial, compressive loads applied at the member ends
keyMemory: Column = vertical member primarily carrying axial compression.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Column
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Column.
Bakit column?
Ito ang textbook structural definition ng column — a vertical compression member carrying primarily axial load.
Bakit hindi post?
Ito ang common trap.
A post is also a vertical support member, yes, but in engineering/formal structural language, the term with the classic definition involving axial compressive load at member ends is column.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Brace stabilizing member iyon.
Lintel horizontal member over opening iyon.
Elimination method
Kapag ang wording ay sobrang textbook at engineering-style:
•	axial
•	compressive loads
•	member ends
Most likely the answer is column.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Column = vertical member primarily carrying axial compression.

question: The part of a foundation system which supports the exterior walls of a superstructure and bears directly on the column footing is a
keyMemory: Sa reviewer distinctions: • Foundation wall = below-grade retaining function • Grade wall = wall support at grade/foundation line
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Grade wall
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Grade wall.
Bakit grade wall?
Base sa wording ng reviewer, ang hinahanap ay below- or near-grade wall member supporting the exterior wall line and bearing on the footings. Dito, the keyed reviewer answer is grade wall.
Bakit hindi foundation wall?
Magkalapit talaga ito, at ito ang trap.
Pero tandaan:
•	Earlier item asked for the permanent retaining wall below grade → foundation wall
•	This one asks for the wall part supporting exterior walls and bearing on footing → in reviewer language, grade wall
Bakit hindi grade beam?
Beam iyon, not wall.
Foundation course not the correct term.
Elimination method
Kapag dalawang magkalapit na terms ang choices, gamitin mo ang wording difference:
•	retaining wall below grade = foundation wall
•	supports exterior wall line at grade/footing relation = grade wall
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer distinctions:
•	Foundation wall = below-grade retaining function
•	Grade wall = wall support at grade/foundation line

question: A beam that projects beyond one or both its support

Bakit cantilever beam?

Projecting beam sounds descriptive but not the standard structural term.

Continuous beam spans across multiple supports, not necessarily projecting beyond support.

question: Refer to the portion of a beam where bending moment changes from positive to negative is?

Deflection point not the correct technical term.
Top beam obviously wrong.

•	positive to negative

Change of bending moment sign = inflection point.

question: When the ratio of short span to the long span of a slab is less than 0.50, slab is a
keyMemory: Short span / long span ratio very small = one-way slab.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. One-way slab
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay One-way slab.
Bakit one-way slab?
Sa slab behavior, kapag ang short span / long span ratio is less than about 0.5, mas dominant ang spanning action in one direction. Kaya one-way slab ang classification.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Two-way slab usually when the ratio is not too small and both directions participate significantly.
Slab-on-fill support condition iyon, not span-ratio classification.
Cantilevered slab different structural condition iyon.
Elimination method
Memory peg:
•	small ratio = one-way
•	closer spans / more square = two-way
So kapag less than 0.50, one-way na iyan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Short span / long span ratio very small = one-way slab.

answerConceptVisual.title: One-way slab rule
answerConceptVisual.detail: If the slab is much longer in one direction, it acts as a one-way slab.

question: A one-way concrete slab is used when

choices.text: the slab is being supported by two parallel beams
the slab has no cantilevered portion
the concrete slab is reinforced on the near bottom side
the concrete slab is reinforced on the near bottom side

answer: a. the slab is being supported by two parallel beams
keyMemory: One-way slab = spans mainly in one direction between parallel supports.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. the slab is being supported by two parallel beams
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay the slab is being supported by two parallel beams.
Bakit ito ang tama?
A one-way slab generally spans in one direction, usually between two parallel supports. Kaya sa choices, iyon ang pinaka-defining condition.
Bakit mali ang iba?
No cantilevered portion hindi iyan ang defining condition for one-way slab.
Reinforced on the near bottom side too vague, and duplicated pa ang c and d; not the main classification basis.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay when is one-way slab used, hanapin ang support condition, not reinforcement wording.
One-way action usually means spanning between two parallel beams/walls.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
One-way slab = spans mainly in one direction between parallel supports.

question: A type of concrete floor which has no beam is called
keyMemory: Beamless concrete floor = flat slab ang classic answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Flat slab
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Flat slab.
Bakit flat slab?
Ang pinaka-keyword dito ay “has no beam.” Sa structural systems, ang flat slab is a slab system supported directly by columns, usually without beams. Minsan may drop panels or column capitals, pero ang core idea niya is beamless slab system.
Bakit mali ang iba?
One-way slab puwedeng may beams.
Two-way slab puwede ring may beams.
Ribbed floor may ribs or joist-like pattern, so hindi ito iyong “no beam” identity na gusto ng tanong.
Elimination method
Kapag may phrase na no beam, huwag agad pumunta sa one-way or two-way dahil slab behavior iyon, not beamless system type.
Hanapin mo ang system na kilalang supported directly by columns: flat slab.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Beamless concrete floor = flat slab ang classic answer.

keyMemory: Shear wall = in-plane lateral load resistance.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Shear wall

Bakit shear wall?
Very textbook ito. A shear wall is specifically designed to resist lateral forces in its own plane — tulad ng wind or earthquake loads.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Bearing wall carries vertical loads mainly.
Curtain wall non-load bearing exterior enclosure iyon.
Grade wall wall term near foundation/grade line iyon, not lateral-resisting primary element.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	lateral forces
•	parallel to plane of wall
Kapag ganyan ang wording, automatic shear wall ang isipin mo.

question: A wall which supports vertical loads in addition to its weight without the benefit of a complete vertical load carrying space frame is called:
keyMemory: Vertical-load-carrying wall = bearing wall.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Bearing wall
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Bearing wall.
Bakit bearing wall?
Ang clue dito ay supports vertical loads in addition to its own weight. Iyan mismo ang definition ng bearing wall. Hindi lang siya enclosure; may dala siyang structural load.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Curtain wall does not carry floor/roof gravity loads except sarili niyang weight.
Retaining wall humahawak ng lupa, hindi main vertical building-load wall.
Shear wall lateral load function ang primary emphasis.
Elimination method
Kapag tanong ay vertical load support by wall, ang first answer mo dapat ay bearing wall.
Kapag lateral load ang clue, saka ka mag-shear wall.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Vertical-load-carrying wall = bearing wall.

A party wall is commonly understood as a wall shared by two adjoining properties or dwelling units. Sa choices, the closest and expected reviewer answer is common wall.
Bakit hindi fire wall?
Ito ang trap. Totoo na sa code context, walls between units may require fire-resistance. Pero ang tanong ay also known as party wall, not specifically asking for fire-resistance classification.
Bakit mali ang iba?

Kapag ang wording ay serves two dwelling units / shared wall, hanapin ang shared-ownership/shared-use term.

Party wall = common wall sa ganitong reviewer wording.

Bakit buttress?
Classic definition ito. A buttress is an external support added to resist lateral or outward thrust, especially in masonry structures.

Column mainly vertical gravity support.

Kapag may phrase na external support plus outward thrust, huwag ka nang lumayo sa buttress.

question: In a steel-framed structure, which of the following does NOT generally provide for required lateral force resistance?
keyMemory: Sa lateral systems, think bracing, rigid frame, shear wall. Pile cap is not the answer there.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Pile caps
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Pile caps.
Bakit pile caps?
Ang pile cap is part of the foundation system distributing loads to piles. Hindi iyan ang usual lateral-force-resisting system ng steel-framed building superstructure.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Actually, sila ang natatanggal kasi sila mismo ang nagbibigay ng lateral resistance:
•	steel bracing yes
•	rigid frame yes
•	shear walls yes
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay may NOT, baliktarin mo ang mindset mo.
Hanapin mo ang hindi kabilang sa lateral-force-resisting systems.
That is pile cap.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa lateral systems, think bracing, rigid frame, shear wall.
Pile cap is not the answer there.

SPACE FRAMES

answerConceptVisual.title: Lateral force resistance
answerConceptVisual.main: Pile caps do NOT generally provide lateral-force resistance for a steel frame

question: Which comes first when finishing a reinforced concrete main staircase
keyMemory: Sa finishing sequence, base preparation comes before final finish and accessories.

Bakit mortar topping?
Sa staircase finishing sequence, kailangan mo munang ihanda at i-level ang surface before final finish and accessories. Ang mortar topping comes before tread/riser finish and before balusters or skirting details.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Stair tread and riser finish after base preparation pa iyan.
Baluster is later pa dahil final finishing/rail-related installation na iyon.
Stair waist stainless skirting finishing trim na iyan, hindi first step.
Elimination method
Kapag tinanong ka ng which comes first in finishing, isipin mo ang sequence:

2.	finish layer
3.	accessories/trim
So first among the choices: mortar topping.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa finishing sequence, base preparation comes before final finish and accessories.

question: Installation of drywall partition: If fiber-cement boards shall be used for the walls separating the administration offices, how many pieces will be use if the wall is 106.00 sq.m.?
keyMemory: Sa estimating items, kapag kulang ang assumptions sa statement, minsan kailangan mong sumunod sa reviewer-standard board size logic.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. 55.0
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 55 pieces.
Bakit 55?
Typical fiber-cement board size in these reviewer computations is usually 1.2 m x 2.4 m, which is 2.88 sq.m per board.
So:
106.00 / 2.88 = 36.8 boards for one side only.
Pero ang tanong says walls separating administration offices — partition wall iyan, so normally dalawang sides ang babalatan.
So:
36.8 × 2 = 73.6 sq.m equivalent boards? Wait, not exact count yet if framing context differs.
But since the available choices include 55.0 and 73.6, reviewer logic here likely assumes a different board module or practical factor.
Let’s use board-review approach: if the expected board size here is 4' x 8' ≈ 32 sq ft ≈ 2.97 sq.m, then
106 / 2.97 = 35.7, still not 55.
Given the provided keyed reviewer-style choices, the most accepted answer for fiber-cement partition boards in these old reviewers for 106 sq.m is 55.0, likely assuming standard sheet coverage around 1.92 sq.m or factoring practical partition application. So within the reviewer context, 55 is the intended answer.
Pero maging honest tayo: medyo ambiguous itong item dahil hindi niya sinabi ang exact board size.
Sa exam strategy, pipiliin mo ang reviewer-standard answer, which is 55.0.
Elimination method
Kapag kulang ang dimensions ng board sa tanong, hanapin ang reviewer-standard expected count, not fully custom computation.
Sa choices, ang 55.0 ang usual keyed answer for this item.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa estimating items, kapag kulang ang assumptions sa statement, minsan kailangan mong sumunod sa reviewer-standard board size logic.

question: Total number of self drilling screw needed to fasten in one gypsum board on metal frames if the spacing of the studs is at 400mm and spacing of screws at vertical is 300mm?
keyMemory: Sa ganitong drywall item, 28 screws ang common reviewer answer for one gypsum board under the given spacing.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 28
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 28.
Bakit 28?
Board-review computation ito based on the standard gypsum board size, usually 1.2m x 2.4m. Kung studs are at 400mm o.c., across a 1.2m width, magkakaroon ka ng screw lines at roughly:
•	left edge
•	400 mm
•	800 mm
•	right edge / 1200 mm
Ibig sabihin, around 4 vertical fastening lines.
Then if screw spacing vertically is 300 mm over a 2.4m height, you get about:
2400 / 300 = 8 intervals, which means around 7 to 8 screw points per line depending on reviewer counting method.
The reviewer-standard count here gives 28 screws per board.
Ito ang mahalagang point:
Sa actual field, puwedeng mag-vary ang exact count depending on whether edges are shared, whether top and bottom are counted the same way, at project specs. Pero sa ALE reviewer style, ang keyed answer sa ganitong setup is 28.
Bakit hindi 24, 32, 36?
24 medyo kulang.
32 and 36 masyadong mataas na for the reviewer pattern.
Ang pinaka-reasonable standard reviewer count is 28.
Elimination method
Kapag gypsum board screw count ang tanong:
1.	Assume standard board size unless otherwise stated
2.	Count likely screw lines based on stud spacing
3.	Count likely screws per line based on vertical spacing
4.	Pick the closest reviewer-standard answer
Common trap sa board exam:
Minsan exact real-world count ang iniisip mo. Pero reviewer questions often use a standard simplified count.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong drywall item, 28 screws ang common reviewer answer for one gypsum board under the given spacing.

answerConceptVisual.title: Gypsum board screw count
answerConceptVisual.detail: Based on 400 mm stud spacing and 300 mm vertical screw spacing.

Compute natin dahan-dahan:
Room area:
6 m × 6 m = 36 sq.m
Standard gypsum board area, reviewer logic:
1.2 m × 2.4 m = 2.88 sq.m per board
Then:
36 / 2.88 = 12.5 boards
Since hindi puwedeng kalahating board lang ang order mo as final sheet count, round up ka.
So kailangan mo ng 13 boards.
Bakit hindi 12?
Kasi 12.5 iyan, so kulang ang 12.
Bakit hindi 10 or 20?
Malayo na sa computation.
Elimination method
Memory peg:
•	room area first
•	board area next
•	divide
•	always round up
Common trap sa board exam:
Maraming examinee ang nag-iiwan sa 12.5 tapos pinipili ang 12 kasi mas malapit. Mali iyon sa materials estimating. Kapag kulang ang sheet, hindi mo makukumpleto ang trabaho.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa board estimating, sheet counts are rounded up, not down.

DOORS

question: What is important when installing aluminum door or window frames?

choices.text: The opening must be square and plumb

answer: a. The opening must be square and plumb
keyMemory: Sa frame installation, opening accuracy comes first. Kung hindi square at plumb ang opening, sablay ang buong installation.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. The opening must be square and plumb
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay The opening must be square and plumb.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Before any frame installation, lalo na aluminum door and window frames, the opening must be true, square, plumb, and properly sized. Kung mali ang opening geometry, kahit gaano kaganda ang frame, magkakaroon ka ng alignment problems, poor fit, leakage, binding, at installation issues.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Thickness of glass important sa design/spec, yes, pero hindi iyan ang pinaka-basic installation requirement ng opening.
Finish of the aluminum frame aesthetic/spec issue iyon.
Cost obviously not an installation criterion.
Elimination method
Kapag installation question, unahin mo ang substrate or opening condition, not appearance or budget.
That is why square and plumb stays.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa frame installation, opening accuracy comes first.
Kung hindi square at plumb ang opening, sablay ang buong installation.

Sige, Page 18 tayo. Maraming practical-site at finishing questions dito, tapos may doors and windows pa. Ang style ng page na ito: maraming items na mukhang simple, pero ang susi talaga ay sequence, standard practice, at elimination by clue words.

question: What is used to bore a hole on a glass?
keyMemory: To drill glass, use a diamond bit. To score/cut glass, diamond cutter naman.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Diamond bit
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Diamond bit.
Bakit diamond bit?
Kapag bore a hole ang tanong, kailangan mo ng tool na talagang pang-drill through glass. Ang tamang tool for that is diamond bit, because glass is brittle and needs a very hard cutting/drilling surface.
Bakit hindi diamond cutter?
Ito ang trap.
A diamond cutter is more associated with scoring/cutting glass, not boring a round hole through it.
Diamond pad pang-polish/abrasive use iyon.
Hammer obvious na mali dahil babasagin lang ang glass.
Elimination method
Keyword alert:
•	bore a hole
•	not cut straight
•	not polish
•	not strike
So:
•	cutter = score/cut
•	pad = polish
•	hammer = break
•	bit = drill/bore
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
To drill glass, use a diamond bit.
To score/cut glass, diamond cutter naman.

question: Best window frame for a vacation house on a hill, fronting the sea?
keyMemory: Sa marine/coastal exposure, corrosion resistance ang unang iniisip mo. Dito, uPVC ang natitira.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. uPVC
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay uPVC.
Bakit uPVC?
Ang clue dito ay fronting the sea. Ibig sabihin, mataas ang chance ng salt-laden air, corrosion, at harsh moisture exposure. Sa choices, uPVC ang pinaka-corrosion-resistant and low-maintenance compared to steel and aluminum in that specific coastal condition.
Bakit hindi steel?
Steel is the first one you should doubt in a marine environment dahil madaling tamaan ng corrosion kapag hindi sobrang well-protected.
Bakit hindi aluminum?
Aluminum is much better than steel in corrosion resistance, yes, pero sa salty coastal exposure, ang reviewer-safe answer for low-maintenance and corrosion resistance is still uPVC.
Bakit hindi all of the above?
Hindi puwedeng “best” lahat. Board trap iyan.
Elimination method
Kapag may phrase na:
•	fronting the sea
•	hill
•	exposed to marine environment
Hanapin mo ang material na:
•	hindi kinakalawang
•	hindi madaling ma-corrode
•	low maintenance
Sa choices, uPVC ang pinaka-board-safe.
Common trap sa board exam:
Pipiliin ng iba ang aluminum dahil “hindi naman bakal.” Pero kapag tinanong ang best in coastal setting, mas malakas ang uPVC.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa marine/coastal exposure, corrosion resistance ang unang iniisip mo.
Dito, uPVC ang natitira.

question: For a wood surface finishing without nail setting. After the application of wood stain, what is next?
keyMemory: Sa wood finishing, after staining, a common next step is sanding sealer before final finish coats.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Apply sanding sealer
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Apply sanding sealer.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang tanong may mahalagang clue: without nail setting. Ibig sabihin, wala kang step na magpa-putty ng nail holes. Tapos sinabi rin na after application of wood stain. Sa normal wood finishing sequence, after stain, a common next step is sanding sealer para ma-seal ang wood fibers and prepare the surface for final coats.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Putty usually comes in relation to defects, holes, or nail setting concerns. Since sinabi nang without nail setting, humihina ito.
Spraying masyadong broad. Ano ang isi-spray? Wala pang na-establish na final or topcoat stage sa tanong.
Apply wood filler mas maaga dapat ito if needed, before stain in many finishing systems.
Elimination method
Ganito ang iniisip mo:
•	no nail setting, so less likely ang putty
•	stain has already been applied, so wood filler is too late
•	“spraying” is not a material step, too vague
•	sealing step logically follows stain
So ang natitira: sanding sealer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa wood finishing, after staining, a common next step is sanding sealer before final finish coats.

question: What is the disadvantage of using skim coats

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang skim coat is a thin finishing layer. Dahil manipis lang siya, hindi niya kayang itago nang husto ang major irregularities. Kaya kailangan mo ng maayos at makinis na plaster base bago siya i-apply.
Bakit mali ang iba?

•	skim coat is thin
•	thin layer means it depends on a good base
So the weakness is not curing time, but the requirement that the substrate/plastering must already be smooth.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Skim coat is not a heavy corrective layer. Kailangan maayos na ang base plaster.

Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 7 days.
Ito ang medyo tricky dahil may previous question on hours. Dito naman days ang tinatanong. Sa reviewer logic, ang more conservative curing/drying before final paint over skim coat is 7 days.
Ito ang common trap sa board exam:
Magkasunod ang tanong:
•	isa in hours
•	isa in days
Kapag hindi ka nagbasa nang mabuti, madadala mo ang previous answer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
12 hours and 24 hours are too short if the question explicitly asks minimum number of days.
14 days may be possible in stricter or wetter conditions, but the keyed reviewer answer is 7 days.
Elimination method
Pag may mixed-unit trap:
•	basahin muna kung hours ba o days
•	huwag basta magdala ng previous answer
Dito, since days ang usapan, the standard minimum answer is 7 days.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Mag-ingat sa unit traps: hours vs days.
Dito, skim coat before paint = 7 days ang reviewer answer.

question: How many gallons of paint as final coat shall be used in the ceiling of a master bedroom if the floor area is 156 sq.m, using the ratio of 1 gallon = 20 sq.m?
keyMemory: Sa estimating questions, distinguish between exact computation and procurement rounding. Dito, exact answer ang kailangan.

Correct answer: b. 7.8 gallons
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay 7.8 gallons.
Compute natin:
Given:
•	Area = 156 sq.m
•	Coverage = 1 gallon : 20 sq.m
So:
156 / 20 = 7.8 gallons
Diretso lang.
Bakit mali ang iba?
6.3 kulang.
8.0 puwedeng rounded-up practical order quantity, pero ang exact computed answer sa choices is 7.8.
8.58 mali computation.
Elimination method
Sa paint estimating:
1.	area
2.	divide by coverage
3.	piliin ang exact computed value unless the question says to round off or order quantity
Dito, walang sinabi na i-round up, kaya 7.8 ang tamang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?

question: A paint defect by discoloration of a coat caused by soluble color in the underlying surface:
keyMemory: Color/stain from below migrating upward = bleeding.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Bleeding
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Bleeding.
Bakit bleeding?
Sa painting defects, bleeding refers to discoloration caused by soluble stain or color from the underlying layer migrating into the top coat.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Spotting is more local mark-like defect.
Blistering is bubble/swelling defect.
Deadening refers more to loss of gloss or dullness.
Elimination method
Keyword:
•	discoloration
•	soluble color
•	underlying surface
That is classic bleeding.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Color/stain from below migrating upward = bleeding.

question: A paint defect described as the swelling of the entire film followed by breaking of the film caused by dampness, moisture and greasy surfaces
keyMemory: Wrinkling = distorted, uneven, shriveled paint film appearance.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Wrinkling
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Wrinkling.
Bakit wrinkling?
Sa reviewer wording, the defect is described as swelling and then breaking/distortion of the paint film due to moisture, greasy surfaces, or improper drying condition. Among the given choices, the expected answer is wrinkling.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Chalking is powdering.
Peeling is loss of adhesion where film comes off.
Checking is formation of small cracks.
The described swollen, distorted film condition best matches wrinkling in this reviewer set.
Elimination method
Kapag defect looks like:
•	film distortion
•	puckering
•	shriveled or uneven surface
Think wrinkling rather than chalking or checking.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Wrinkling = distorted, uneven, shriveled paint film appearance.

The phrase progressive powdering from the surface inward is textbook chalking. The binder degrades and the surface releases powder.

Blistering bubble defect.
Checking hairline cracks.
Cracking larger fissures or breaks.

question: A common paint problem characterized by presence of loose, removable powder evolved from the film itself. This may be detected by rubbing the film with the fingertip.
keyMemory: Sa paint defects, kapag powder ang lumalabas sa daliri, ang sagot ay chalking.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Chalking
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Chalking.
Bakit chalking ulit?
Kasi parehong-pareho ang clue:
•	loose removable powder
•	comes from the paint film itself
•	detected by rubbing with fingertip
That is classic chalking.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Blistering bubbles.
Blushing cloudy or milky appearance, often in lacquer due to moisture.
Cracking cracks, not powder.
Elimination method
Kapag sinabi ng tanong na pwede mong i-rub and may powder, huwag ka nang lumayo sa chalking.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa paint defects, kapag powder ang lumalabas sa daliri, ang sagot ay chalking.

question: A paint defect of inferior brands of paints where the soluble pigments soften and dissolves by water or by chemicals even with sufficient drying is called:
keyMemory: Pigment dissolves or washes away = washing. Huwag mo itong ipagkamali sa bleeding.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Washing
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Washing.
Bakit washing?
Ang clue dito ay:
•	soluble pigments
•	soften and dissolve by water or chemicals
•	kahit may sufficient drying na
Ibig sabihin, ang problema ay nahuhugasan o natatanggal ang pigment film. Kaya ang tamang defect term dito ay washing.
Bakit hindi bleeding?
Ito ang common trap.
Bleeding is migration of color from an underlying surface into the top coat.
Dito, hindi color migration ang issue — ang issue ay natutunaw o nahuhugasan ang pigment mismo.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Wrinkling is distortion/shriveling of paint film.
Fading is gradual loss of color, usually from exposure, not dissolving by water/chemicals.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa paint defect:
•	natutunaw / nahuhugasan = washing
•	lumilipat ang kulay mula sa ilalim = bleeding
•	kumukupas = fading
•	kulubot/distorted film = wrinkling
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Pigment dissolves or washes away = washing.
Huwag mo itong ipagkamali sa bleeding.

question: What is the cause of coagulation of paint in a can?
keyMemory: Kapag paint problem is inside the can, think first of storage deterioration or age.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Age
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Age.
Bakit age?
Ang coagulation in a can means nag-clump or naghiwalay na ang paint components habang naka-store. The most basic and classic cause in reviewer logic is aging or over-storage. Habang tumatagal, puwedeng mag-thicken, mag-separate, o mag-coagulate ang paint.
Bakit hindi lack of thinner?
Hindi iyan ang pangunahing reason why paint coagulates while in the can. Puwede lang maging thick ang consistency, pero ang tanong ay cause of coagulation.
Poor application happens after the paint is already used, so hindi relevant sa “in a can.”
Exposure to direct sunlight can worsen storage condition, yes, pero the clean and expected root answer dito is age.
Elimination method
Keyword:
•	in a can
Ibig sabihin, storage issue ito, not application issue.
So tanggal agad ang:
•	poor application
Then compare:
•	lack of thinner
•	direct sunlight
•	age
The most direct, standard answer is age.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag paint problem is inside the can, think first of storage deterioration or age.

Ceiling framing has joists, furring, channels, and similar members, not primarily studs.

stud = wall

question: Are used to support the ceiling surface material?
keyMemory: Ceiling finish materials are commonly attached to furring.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Furring
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Furring.
Bakit furring?
The ceiling surface material — lalo na lightweight finishes like boards or panels — is often attached to furring members. Ang furring is the non-structural support layer that levels and provides attachment points for ceiling finish.
Bakit hindi ceiling joist?
Ito ang common trap.
Ceiling joist is a structural member, yes, but the question is asking what is used to support the ceiling surface material. Ang mas direct finish-support member dito is furring.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Studs are for walls.
Joist too general and more structural than finish-support in this context.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa surface material, huwag agad pumili ng primary structural member.
Hanapin mo ang finish-support/attachment member.
Dito iyon si furring.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Ceiling finish materials are commonly attached to furring.

question: Refers to a non-structural strip of wood or metal used to level a surface?
keyMemory: Furring = non-structural strip for leveling/supporting finishes.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Furring
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Furring.
Bakit furring?
Ito mismo ang definition ng furring — non-structural strips of wood or metal used to level surfaces or provide a base for finish materials.
Bakit hindi batten?
Batten is also a strip, yes, but usually used to cover joints or support tiles/boards depending on context. The key phrase here is non-structural strip used to level a surface — that is classic furring.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Studs and joists are structural framing members, not non-structural leveling strips.
Elimination method
Hanapin ang dalawang clue:
•	non-structural
•	level a surface
That immediately removes studs and joists.
Between batten and furring, furring is the better match.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Furring = non-structural strip for leveling/supporting finishes.

question: Who is responsible for the MEPF perforations on moisture resistant acoustic boards?

explanation:
Correct answer: a. MEPF subcontractors

Bakit MEPF subcontractors?
Kung ang perforations or openings are for mechanical, electrical, plumbing, fire protection requirements, then the trade responsible for those service penetrations is the MEPF subcontractor, since sila ang may exact requirements for grills, diffusers, fixtures, pipes, and other service devices.
Bakit hindi acoustic board subcontractor?
They install the boards, yes, but the openings for MEPF are driven by the MEPF layout and coordination.

Who owns the service layout and devices?
That is the MEPF trade.

Service penetrations belong to the service trade — dito, MEPF subcontractors.

question: For Toilet. Ideally, which should be installed first. wall tile or floor tile?
keyMemory: Sa T&B, wall tile first is the usual ideal finishing sequence answer.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Wall Tile
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Wall Tile.
Bakit wall tile muna?
Sa ideal sequence, wall tiles are commonly installed first so the floor finish can remain cleaner, protected, and better aligned at the final base condition. This also helps avoid damaging finished floor tiles while wall works are ongoing.
Bakit hindi floor tile muna?
Common trap ito dahil iniisip ng iba na base muna. Pero in actual finishing sequence, finishing the floor too early can expose it to adhesive drops, debris, and damage from wall tile work.
Either and same time are too loose and not ideal.
Elimination method
Kapag toilet and bath finishing sequence:
•	think about protecting the finished floor
•	think about cleaner wall-to-floor termination
That leads to wall tile first.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa T&B, wall tile first is the usual ideal finishing sequence answer.

answerConceptVisual.title: Toilet tile sequence
answerConceptVisual.detail: Wall tile first protects the finished floor tile from damage and mess during wall works.

question: When monitoring or supervising a particular project, three concerns should be considered. These are Planning, Execution and?
keyMemory: Sa supervision logic: Planning, Execution, Checking. Iyan ang mabilis na memory peg mo.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Checking
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Checking.
Bakit checking?
Sa project monitoring and supervision, ang normal flow ng control thinking ay:
•	Planning — ano ang gagawin, paano gagawin, kailan gagawin
•	Execution — actual na paggawa o implementation
•	Checking — pag-verify kung tama, on schedule, pasok sa quality, at ayon sa plano
Iyan ang pinaka-logical trio. Parang management/control cycle din ang dating: plan, do, check.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Recording importante, oo, pero hindi iyan ang pinaka-core third concern after planning and execution sa ganitong reviewer wording.
Purchasing masyadong narrow; procurement activity iyan, hindi general supervision concern.
Testing puwedeng parte ng QC, pero mas specific iyan. Ang tanong ay broad supervision concern, kaya mas umbrella term ang checking.
Elimination method
Ganito mag-isip:
•	Ang tanong ay broad management/supervision concern, hindi specific technical activity
•	So huwag piliin ang masyadong makitid tulad ng purchasing or testing
•	Hanapin ang partner ng planning and execution sa control cycle
That leaves checking.
Common ALE trap ito:
Kapag nakita mo ang word na “monitoring,” matetempt kang piliin ang recording or testing. Pero ang mas buo at mas general na concept ay checking.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa supervision logic: Planning, Execution, Checking.
Iyan ang mabilis na memory peg mo.

answerConceptVisual.title: Project monitoring
answerConceptVisual.detail: The third concern in supervision/monitoring is checking.

question: In this contract arrangement the Contractor carries out all engineering, procurement and construction, providing a fully equipped and ready for immediate use facility?
keyMemory: Kapag ang facility ay fully equipped and ready for immediate use, ang sagot ay Turnkey.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Turnkey
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Turnkey.
Bakit turnkey?
Ito ang pinaka-importanteng clue sa tanong:
“fully equipped and ready for immediate use facility”
Iyan na iyan ang signature phrase ng turnkey contract.
Kapag turnover time na, parang iikot mo na lang ang susi at puwede nang gamitin agad ang facility. Kaya nga “turnkey.”
Bakit hindi design-build?
Ito ang common trap.
Sa design-build, the contractor handles both design and construction, yes. Pero hindi laging ipinapahiwatig ng term na iyon na fully equipped and ready for immediate operation/use ang facility.
Ang turnkey mas complete ang promise: design, procure, construct, equip, then turnover as ready-to-use.
Bakit hindi BOT?
BOT means Build-Operate-Transfer. Diyan may period pa na o-operate muna ng private entity bago i-transfer. Hindi iyan ang simpleng immediate turnover arrangement na hinahanap dito.
Bakit hindi fast track?
Fast track is about schedule overlap — construction starts before design is fully complete. Hindi ito contract arrangement defined by ready-for-immediate-use turnover.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
•	Ready for immediate use? → Turnkey
•	Contractor does design + build only? → Design-Build
•	Build, operate, then transfer later? → BOT
•	Construction starts before design is fully completed? → Fast Track
Kapag minaster mo itong apat na distinction, ang dami mong masasagutan sa ALE.
Quick memory shortcut
•	Turnkey = ready to use
•	Design-Build = one entity for design and construction
•	BOT = private sector builds, operates, then transfers
•	Fast Track = overlap ng design at construction for speed
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang facility ay fully equipped and ready for immediate use, ang sagot ay Turnkey.

Sige, Page 25 tayo. Isang visible question lang ang nandito sa ibaba, pero magandang item ito kasi sinusukat nito kung marunong kang kumilala ng structural system by application.

answerConceptVisual.title: Contract arrangement
answerConceptVisual.detail: Contractor delivers an engineered, procured, constructed, fully equipped, ready-to-use facility.

question: What do a bridge, MRT and a long horizontal structure have one thing in common?
answer: a. All used a space frame
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay bridge, MRT, long-span horizontal structure, ang reviewer-intended answer dito ay space frame / long-span structural system logic, hindi “lahat bakal” o “lahat may seismic gap.”
explanation:
Correct answer: a. All used a space frame
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay All used a space frame.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue dito ay bridge, MRT, and long horizontal structure. Ang common denominator na gustong kunin ng reviewer ay iyong structural system for spanning large distances efficiently. Sa ganitong reviewer logic, the intended answer is space frame because it is associated with long-span, efficient, interconnected structural action.
Ngayon, maging maingat tayo rito.
Sa totoong practice, hindi lahat ng bridges at MRT structures ay literally “space frame” in the strictest engineering sense. Maraming bridge types: girder bridges, trusses, box girders, segmental systems, etc. So kung sobrang literal at real-world-specific ang tanong, puwedeng i-challenge ang wording. Pero sa ALE reviewer logic, ang gustong sagot dito ay iyong long-span structural concept, and among the choices, space frame ang pinaka-fit.
Bakit hindi all are steel structure?
Ito ang common trap.
Hindi lahat ng bridge o MRT structure ay steel. Maraming reinforced concrete, prestressed concrete, composite, etc. Kaya masyadong absolute ang statement na all are steel structure.
Bakit hindi all has a seismic gap?
Hindi rin puwedeng absolute. Some structures may require movement joints or seismic separation depending on design, but hindi ito ang universal defining feature ng tatlo.
Bakit hindi all needs to be painted?
Obviously too broad and too weak. Hindi structural commonality iyan. May ibang structures na hindi painted finish ang primary treatment.
Elimination method
Ganito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, alisin mo agad ang mga overly absolute but obviously weak:
•	all needs to be painted → hindi ito meaningful structural identity
Pangalawa, alisin ang too-specific but not universally true:
•	all are steel structure → mali, kasi puwedeng concrete or composite
•	all has a seismic gap → not always the defining common feature
So ang natitira:
•	all used a space frame
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices — ito ang pinaka-malapit sa reviewer’s intended long-span structural idea.
Common ALE trap
Minsan ang tanong ay hindi perfectly worded in strict engineering terms. Kapag reviewer-style item at halatang ang intention ay “anong system ang associated sa long horizontal spanning structure?”, piliin mo ang best available answer, hindi iyong sobrang literal na real-world exception-based reasoning.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay bridge, MRT, long-span horizontal structure, ang reviewer-intended answer dito ay space frame / long-span structural system logic, hindi “lahat bakal” o “lahat may seismic gap.”
Sige, Page 26 tayo. Ito magandang page kasi marami ritong site-practice at board-style practical judgment questions. Dito madalas lumalabas ang tanong na, “Ano ang next best move sa actual construction?” Kaya ang focus natin ay sequence, logic, at elimination.

question: In waterproofing a Roof Deck, up to where is the extent of the waterproofing application? whole floor of the balcony up to the adjacent wall connected at least 12 inches from the finish floor line.

choices.text: along the whole area of the floor of the balcony
along the perimeter of the balcony
along the whole perimeter wall and the whole floor of the balcony

answer: c. along the whole perimeter wall and the whole floor of the balcony
keyMemory: Sa balcony/roof deck waterproofing, hindi floor lang. Dapat may upturn sa adjacent/perimeter wall.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. along the whole perimeter wall and the whole floor of the balcony
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay along the whole perimeter wall and the whole floor of the balcony.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa roof deck or balcony waterproofing, hindi sapat na sa floor lang maglagay. Kailangan may turn-up sa adjacent wall/perimeter wall, usually at least several inches above finished floor level. Kaya ang tamang complete answer ay:
•	buong floor area
•	plus perimeter wall upturn
Bakit hindi a?
Kulang, kasi floor lang.
Bakit hindi b?
Kulang din, wall perimeter lang ang dating.
Bakit hindi d all of the above?
Kasi ang a at b individually are incomplete descriptions. Hindi puwedeng all of the above kung may isang mas complete answer na tama at ang iba ay kulang.
Elimination method
Kapag waterproofing extent ang tanong, isipin mo lagi:
•	horizontal surface ba lang?
•	or pati vertical turn-up?
Kapag balcony or roof deck, ang tamang detail ay floor + wall upturn.
So the most complete answer is c.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa balcony/roof deck waterproofing, hindi floor lang. Dapat may upturn sa adjacent/perimeter wall.

answerConceptVisual.title: Roof deck waterproofing extent
answerConceptVisual.main: Whole perimeter wall + whole balcony floor
answerConceptVisual.detail: Waterproofing should return up the adjacent perimeter wall and cover the whole floor area.

question: What needs to be done to avoid differential settlement of the soil and prevent cracks on slab on fill?

choices.text: Proper compaction of the soil ideally at every 4-6 inches thick fill

answer: b. Proper compaction of the soil ideally at every 4-6 inches thick fill
keyMemory: Para maiwasan ang slab-on-fill cracking due to settlement, proper compaction of fill ang unang sagot, hindi dagdag bakal agad.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Proper compaction of the soil ideally at every 4-6 inches thick fill
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Proper compaction of the soil ideally at every 4-6 inches thick fill.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang root cause ng differential settlement sa slab-on-fill ay madalas nasa subgrade or fill preparation, hindi sa slab steel lang. Kaya ang pinakaimportanteng preventive step ay proper layer-by-layer compaction of the fill.
Bakit hindi additional reinforcement?
Reinforcement can help control cracking behavior, pero hindi nito inaayos ang underlying settlement problem.
Bakit hindi gravel?
Helpful ang gravel sa drainage, pero hindi iyon substitute for proper compaction.
Bakit hindi thicker slab?
Mas mahal lang at hindi pa rin nito masosolve ang poor fill preparation.
Elimination method
Tanungin mo:
Ano ba ang ugat ng differential settlement?
•	slab ba?
•	bakal ba?
•	o lupa/fill?
Kapag soil/fill ang problema, ang sagot dapat nasa soil preparation, not just structural thickening.
So compaction ang natitira.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para maiwasan ang slab-on-fill cracking due to settlement, proper compaction of fill ang unang sagot, hindi dagdag bakal agad.

answerConceptVisual.title: Prevent differential settlement
answerConceptVisual.main: Compact soil every 4–6 inches of fill
answerConceptVisual.detail: Proper layer-by-layer compaction reduces slab-on-fill settlement and cracking.

question: What needs to be done when pouring concrete in two setting?

choices.text: Cure the first phase first before pouring the second one

keyMemory: Sa two-stage concreting, dowels help provide continuity and connection between pours.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Provide dowels
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Provide dowels.
Bakit dowels?
Kapag may two-stage pour or construction joint situation, kailangan mo ng load transfer and mechanical continuity between the first and second pour. One standard answer is provide dowels.
Bakit hindi cure first before second?
Puwede mangyari in sequence, pero hindi iyan ang key construction detail being asked.
Bakit hindi shoring?
Not the direct answer sa continuity between pours.
Bakit hindi additional reinforcement?
Too vague. The specific detail for connecting two pours is dowels.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay pouring in two setting, isipin mo agad:
“How do I connect old and new concrete?”
That points to dowels and joint treatment.

question: A friend of yours ask you to design the additional floor of their 20 year old 2-storey house which is originally designed as a 3 storey house. Due to budget constraint they just manage to construct two floors of the house. Wondering about the structural integrity of the house, you consult a Structural Engineer to check the structure. Upon investigation of the Structural Engineer, the additional floor might not be possible if it will just rely on the existing condition of the house with several issues on the columns and the fact that it’s already 20 year old house. But as per him, it is still possible. In order to “push through” with the construction of the additional floor, what needs to be done first?

choices.text: Demolished and construct a new house
Just continue constructing the additional floors however by using lightweight materials.

keyMemory: Kapag may old structure na may issues, strengthen first before adding storeys. Board-safe answer: retrofit.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Retrofit the house
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Retrofit the house.
Bakit retrofit?
Ang scenario mismo nagsabi:
•	old house
•	existing structural issues
•	not advisable to rely on current condition alone
•	still possible if intervention is made
That intervention is retrofitting or structural strengthening before additional loading.
Bakit hindi add columns agad?
Possible as part of retrofitting strategy, yes, but too narrow and not always the first umbrella answer.
Bakit hindi demolish?
Sobrang absolute, and the scenario says possible pa.
Bakit hindi just use lightweight materials?
Delikado iyan bilang casual answer kung may existing structural issues na.
Elimination method
Kapag may existing structural deficiency and gusto pang dagdagan ang load, ang unang iniisip ay:
•	strengthen first
•	assess and retrofit
•	saka magdagdag
So the broad, correct first move is retrofit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may old structure na may issues, strengthen first before adding storeys.
Board-safe answer: retrofit.

answerConceptVisual.title: Structural upgrade
answerConceptVisual.detail: For an added floor on an old structure with column issues, structural retrofitting comes before construction.

question: What will you use if you want a column-free space or to minimize the use of columns on your designed building?

Bakit space frame?
Kapag column-free or minimal-column space ang target, ang hinahanap mo ay long-span structural system. Sa choices, space frame construction ang pinaka-direct long-span answer.
Bakit hindi post-tensioning?
Possible rin siya for long spans, yes. Ito ang trap. Pero if the question is asking the strongest, most obvious system for large clear spans and minimizing columns, space frame is the cleaner reviewer answer.

Too broad. Not all steel systems are column-free.
Bakit hindi monolithic or flexible frame?
Not the standard direct answer.
Elimination method
Keyword:
•	column-free
•	minimize columns
•	designed building
That is classic long-span system logic.
Among the choices, space frame construction is the most specific and strongest answer.

For large clear spaces with minimal columns, think space frame first.

question: If the building used precast Single-tee and Double-tee joist what does it eliminates?
keyMemory: Single-tee and double-tee precast members can eliminate separate beam members in reviewer logic.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. beam
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Beam.
Bakit beam?
Single-tee and double-tee precast members act as long-span floor/roof elements that integrate slab-like and stem action. In practical reviewer logic, they reduce or eliminate the need for many intermediate beams.
Bakit hindi slab?
Actually, tees themselves form part of the slab/floor system, so hindi slab ang inaalis.
Bakit hindi column or girder?
They may reduce some support framing demand, but the direct reviewer answer is that they eliminate beams as separate members in many layouts.
Wall obviously not.
Elimination method
Tanungin mo:
Ano ang napapalitan ng tee members?
•	They are not replacing walls
•	not primarily removing columns
•	not removing the slab itself
•	they often function as spanning members that reduce separate beam need
So beam ang natitira.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Single-tee and double-tee precast members can eliminate separate beam members in reviewer logic.

question: Ideal type of footing for sloping site?
answer: b. Isolated with footing tie beam
keyMemory: Sa sloping site, reviewer-safe answer: isolated footings tied by footing tie beams.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Isolated with footing tie beam
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Isolated with footing tie beam.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa sloping sites, common strategy ang paggamit ng isolated footings stepped or adjusted to levels, tied together with tie beams for stability and coordination across varying elevations.
Bakit hindi mat?
Mat foundation is for poor soil/high load conditions, not automatically the ideal for slope.
Bakit hindi cantilever or combined footing?
Those respond more to column spacing/property line eccentricity issues.
Bakit hindi strip footing?
Not the most flexible answer for slope conditions.
Elimination method
Keyword:
•	sloping site
Hanapin ang footing system that can adapt to level differences and still stay tied as one structural system.
That points to isolated footing with tie beam.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa sloping site, reviewer-safe answer: isolated footings tied by footing tie beams.

question: Heavy timber planked joined together horizontally to retain the face of the excavation.

Bakit lagging?

Bakit mali ang iba?
Wales are horizontal members too, pero sila ang structural braces/support members na sumasalo sa load from lagging or sheeting. Hindi sila iyong timber planks na direktang humahawak sa soil face.
Rakers are diagonal braces.
Tie back anchoring system iyan, not timber planking.

•	planked
•	retain the face of excavation
Hanapin mo ang member na direktang nasa soil face.
That is lagging.

Lagging = timber planks against excavation face.
Wales = horizontal support member behind/with the lagging.

question: For a two-storey, 2 bedroom residential building located near beach. Which of the following is the most practical?

choices.text: Isolated footing with footing tie beam

answer: a. Isolated footing with footing tie beam
keyMemory: Sa modest framed residential buildings, isolated footing with tie beam is a strong practical answer, especially when better footing integration is desired.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Isolated footing with footing tie beam
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Isolated footing with footing tie beam.
Bakit ito ang tama?
For a modest two-storey residential building, the practical and economical footing solution in many ordinary conditions is isolated footings. Since it is near the beach, the reviewer likely prefers a more integrated footing system by tying footings together with tie beams for stability and coordination.
Bakit hindi isolated footing lang?
That is the trap.
Possible siya, yes, but with footing tie beam is the more robust and practical reviewer answer, especially with possible site variability near beach areas.
Bakit hindi mat footing?
Too heavy and expensive for a typical two-storey 2-bedroom residence unless soil is especially poor.
Bakit hindi combined footing?
Not the default practical answer unless columns are close or there are boundary issues.
Bakit hindi strip footing?
Less likely as the best general answer if the structure is framed with columns.
Elimination method
Think:
•	small residential building = not mat footing
•	framed building = isolated footing is likely
•	near beach / need better integration = tie beams help
So the best practical answer is isolated footing with footing tie beam.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa modest framed residential buildings, isolated footing with tie beam is a strong practical answer, especially when better footing integration is desired.

Sige, Page 28 tayo. Maraming practical construction at professional-responsibility questions dito, tapos may drainage membranes at pattern identification pa. Dahan-dahan tayo, in order, at lagi nating hahanapin ang pinaka-exact na clue.

Sa toilets, kung light partition ang gagamitin, protektahan muna ang base. Dito, concrete zocalo ang reviewer answer.

question: In installing steel casement window, what should be done first?
answer: c. Opening should be rough finish
keyMemory: Window and door frames are usually installed in prepared rough openings before final finish.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Opening should be rough finish
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Opening should be rough finish.
Bakit rough finish?
Sa window installation, lalo na sa steel casement, the opening is usually prepared first in its rough opening condition before final finishing. The frame is installed, aligned, fixed, and then saka pa lang idi-detail ang surrounding finish.
Bakit hindi opening already finish?
Ito ang common trap. Kapag finish na agad ang opening bago frame installation, mas mahirap ang adjustment at mas risky ang damage sa finished edges.
Bakit hindi dowels?
Possible as part of fixing details, but not the first general condition being asked.
Bakit hindi reinforced concrete?
Not all openings need to be described that way; too broad and not the direct first step answer.
Elimination method
Kapag frame installation ang tanong, isipin mo:
•	rough opening first
•	frame alignment and fixing next
•	final finishing later
So rough finish ang pinaka-logical.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Window and door frames are usually installed in prepared rough openings before final finish.

question: During construction, ideally who prepares reinforcing bar cutting list?
keyMemory: Contractor prepares the rebar cutting list; structural engineer approves/checks it.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Contractor
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Contractor.
Bakit contractor?
During actual construction, the bar cutting list is commonly prepared by the contractor or contractor’s technical staff/fabricator based on the approved structural plans and details. It is part of the contractor’s means of executing and fabricating the rebars.
Bakit hindi structural engineer?
Ito ang trap.
The structural engineer designs and details the reinforcement in the plans, but the actual cutting list/shop list used on site is generally prepared by the contractor for fabrication and scheduling, subject to review/approval as needed.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Architect does not prepare rebar cutting lists.
Owner definitely not.
Elimination method
Separate these two roles in your mind:
•	design/detail origin = structural engineer
•	fabrication/shop preparation for execution = contractor
So the preparer is contractor.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Contractor prepares the rebar cutting list; structural engineer approves/checks it.

answerConceptVisual.title: Rebar cutting list

question: The structural design of a 2-Storey residential building is a responsibility of?
keyMemory: Sa Philippine practice, structural design = Civil Engineer / Structural Engineer.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Civil Engineer
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Civil Engineer.
Bakit civil engineer?
Sa Philippine professional practice, the structural design of a building falls under the responsibility of the civil engineer, specifically the structural engineer function under civil engineering practice.
Bakit hindi architect?
Ito ang importanteng ALE distinction.
The architect is responsible for architectural planning and design, coordination, and allied responsibilities, but the structural design calculations and structural system design are the responsibility of the civil/structural engineer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Foreman and contractor execute/manage construction, not prepare structural design as professional responsibility.
Elimination method
Kapag ang tanong ay structural design, do not pick who builds it. Pick who is professionally licensed and responsible for structural calculations and design.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa Philippine practice, structural design = Civil Engineer / Structural Engineer.

answerConceptVisual.detail: For this reviewer item, structural design of a 2-storey residence is assigned to the Civil Engineer.

question: Which of the following is the most expensive?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, bored pile is often the most expensive pile option.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Bored Pile
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Bored Pile.
Bakit bored pile?
Sa general construction cost logic, bored piles tend to be among the more expensive foundation pile systems because of specialized drilling equipment, slurry/casing possibilities, spoil removal, and more controlled construction process.
Bakit hindi sheet pile?
Sheet piles are retaining/excavation support systems; ibang function at hindi ito ang most likely answer sa pile-cost comparison item.
Bakit hindi driven or hammered pile?
Generally more production-based and often less costly per unit than bored piling in many ordinary situations.
Elimination method
Kapag tinanong ang most expensive and choices include bored pile, malakas lagi iyon dahil mas specialized ang process.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, bored pile is often the most expensive pile option.

question: Which of the following uses Auger Driver?
keyMemory: Auger = boring/drilling, therefore bored pile.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Bored Pile
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Bored Pile.
Bakit bored pile?
An auger is a drilling tool. Bored piles are constructed by drilling/boring into the ground, so auger use is directly associated with bored piles.
Bakit hindi sheet pile or driven pile?
Those are typically driven into the ground, not drilled with auger.
Bakit hindi caisson pile?
Caissons have different sinking/excavation methods; the clean reviewer match for auger is still bored pile.
Elimination method
Keyword:
•	auger
•	boring/drilling
That points directly to bored pile.

question: Which of the following uses floating installation?
keyMemory: Floating installation = engineered wood/laminate plank logic.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Engineer Wood Plank
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Engineered Wood Plank.
Bakit engineered wood plank?
Floating installation is very commonly associated with engineered wood flooring systems where planks lock together and are not directly nailed or glued to the substrate in the traditional way.
Bakit hindi granite or ceramic tile?
Tiles are usually set in mortar or adhesive beds, not floating-installed.
Bakit hindi carpet?
Carpet can be loose-laid or stretched, but the term floating installation is strongly associated with engineered wood/laminate plank systems.
Elimination method
Kapag flooring-installation term ang tanong at may wood plank option, floating system usually points to engineered wood or laminate-type flooring.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Floating installation = engineered wood/laminate plank logic.

question: Which of the following is an accessory for ceiling joist hanger?

explanation:

Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay metal inserts.
Bakit metal inserts?
Sa suspended ceiling or ceiling hanger systems, ang metal inserts are embedded or fixed into the concrete slab/structure to receive hanger wires or suspension components. Among the choices, they are the clearest accessory associated with the hanger anchorage.
Bakit hindi chair?
Chair is for rebar support.
Bakit hindi furring clip?
Clip siya for framing connection, but not the primary hanger-accessory answer here.
Bakit hindi u-channel?
Channel is a framing member, not the accessory/anchorage piece for the hanger itself.

Kapag ceiling hanger ang tanong, isipin mo:
•	saan kakapit ang hanger?
•	anong accessory ang nasa slab/structure to receive it?
That points to metal inserts.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa suspended ceiling support, metal inserts are common hanger anchorage accessories.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Jigsaw = handheld power saw with a narrow vertical reciprocating blade.

Bakit jigsaw?
Makikita sa image na hand-held power tool siya na may vertical up-and-down blade sa harap. Iyan ang pinaka-classic na porma ng jigsaw. Ginagamit ito for curved cuts, irregular cuts, at light panel cutting.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Hack saw hand saw iyon, usually for metal, C-shaped frame.
Backsaw manual saw iyon na may stiffened back.
Rip saw hand saw din iyon for cutting wood along the grain.
Lahat ng ibang choices ay manual saws, samantalang ang image ay clearly power tool.

Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
•	power tool ba ito? oo

So hindi puwedeng hacksaw, backsaw, o rip saw.
Natitira: jigsaw.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Jigsaw = handheld power saw with a narrow vertical reciprocating blade.

question: What should be used in order to provide a structural connection between the poured in place concrete slab and the structural steel.
keyMemory: Steel beam + concrete slab composite action = shear studs.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Shear Studs
Ulit: ang tamang sagot ay Shear Studs.
Bakit shear studs?
Kapag may poured-in-place concrete slab na kailangang maging structurally composite with structural steel, ang standard connector ay shear studs. Sila ang nagta-transfer ng shear between steel beam and concrete slab para mag-behave sila as one composite member.
Ito ang clue:
between concrete slab and structural steel
Hindi lang simpleng connection ang hanap. Structural connection ang sabi. Ibig sabihin, may force transfer involved.
Bakit mali ang iba?
Pins are generic and not the standard slab-to-steel composite connector.
Dowels are more common in concrete-to-concrete joints or paving/slab joints.
Gang nails are for wood truss/wood connections, hindi para sa steel-concrete composite action.
Elimination method
Ganito ang iniisip mo:
•	steel to concrete composite ba? yes
•	wood connector ba? tanggal gang nails
•	concrete to concrete ba? hindi, so tanggal dowels
•	generic pin ba? too weak
Natitira: shear studs
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Steel beam + concrete slab composite action = shear studs.
